wp:term.wp:term_name: avocado oil or extra virgin olive oil
avocado oil or other healthy cooking oil
baby bok choy, washed and halved or quartered lengthwise
Bell peppers (pick the colors you prefer, I always enjoy red and orange)
Canned chipotle peppers in adobo sauce
Chili Oil (Depending how spicy you like it)
cooked sushi rice or short-grain rice
dried chickpeas or 2 cans, drained and rinsed
Fresh cilantro, a dollop of sour cream, a squeeze of lime juice or some fresh jalapeños for a little kick.
large tomato diced or use about 4 tablespoons of crushed tomato sauce
low sodium soy sauce or tamari
Red pepper flakes or hot sauce
Salt and Pepper to taste for the chicken
Slices of sourdough or whole grain bread

Do not include any pre-amble, notes, or post-article commentary.

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Healthy Eating Starts at home

Transform Your Health With Expert Guidance

Start Your Wellness Journey
Live Life Your Way
iOS App — Coming Soon

Your health,
finally decoded.

Labs, sleep, fitness & biomarkers — all in one AI-powered dashboard. Built for people who take their health seriously.

🧬 Lab OCR import ❤️ Biomarker tracking ✦ AI assistant ⌚ Oura sync
Join the Waitlist Learn more →
Health Score
84
↑ 3 pts today
Sleep7h 12m
HRV67 ms
GlucoseOptimal

Principles To Live By

01

Mind

Explore your mind as a new world within yourself, it will take you places only you could have imagined.

02

Nutrition

Nothing tastes as good as healthy feels.

03

Fun Recipes

Life is full of flavor, embrace the sweet, spicy, bitter and sour as each adds it's own depth to your palette.

04

Just Keep Moving

Moving is celebrating what your body can do so make every day count.

05

Strong Connections

Embrace your community, it is what will be your light when the world seems dark.

06

Grow By Learning

Embrace every day as an opportunity to learn something new it is the real fountain of youth.

Improve your well being with an active lifestyle

Live Life to the Fullest: Inactivity can limit your ability to enjoy activities you love, whether it's playing with your kids, going on hikes, or simply keeping up with daily tasks. Staying active allows you to embrace life to the fullest and create lasting memories.

Small amounts of activity are better than none! Just start and gradually increase the intensity and duration of your workouts. Find activities you enjoy, whether it's dancing, swimming, hiking, or team sports. Make movement a part of your lifestyle, and reap the amazing benefits for years to come!

Read More

Protein is a Super Star Nutrient

Protein is the body's building blocks, It's essential for building and repairing tissues, including muscles, bones, skin, and hair. Let's explore some exquisite ways we can incorporate it into our lives.

Read More

Set your own goals, find the new you

10 lbs

Weight Lost

10K

Daily Steps Taken

3

New Healthy Recipes

6

Fitness Challenges Accomplished

Mindfulness

How Do I Start?

Meditation isn't about emptying your mind; it's about training it to watch thoughts float by like leaves on a stream.

Start a guided meditation with apps such as Headspace, Calm, Insight Timer or just find a quite space and just take a few breaths in and out. Meditation is a skill that takes time and practice to develop so make it a habit.

My Mind Just Wont Stop?

This is probably the most frequent complaint! Our minds are naturally busy, and it takes practice to train them to focus. Don't get discouraged – a wandering mind is a normal part of the process. The key is to gently bring your attention back to your breath or your mantra whenever you realize your mind has strayed.

How Do I Know This is Working?

Everyone is different so it will vary but ask yourself

Do you find yourself reacting less strongly to stressful situations or negative emotions?

Are you better able to manage difficult emotions like anger, frustration, or anxiety?

How do you feel after just 5 deep breaths?

I Don't Have Time?

Meditation is like mental hygiene, even ten minutes can change your day. Invest in yourself - a few minutes of peace can create a ripple effect of positivity throughout your life.

Read More

Smoothie Recipes

Objective is to hit your nutrition goals tastefully.

Goals

  • Quick and easy
  • Customizable Nutrition
  • Increased Fruit and Vegetable Intake
  • Dietary Needs Friendly

Quick Breakfast Criteria

  • 24g of Protein
  • 1-2 low sugar fruits for natural sweetness and plant polyphenols
  • 2-3 vegetables for essential vitamins, antioxidants and fiber
  • Mix in some flaxseeds for omega-3s to reduce inflammation
  • A dose of curcumin for anti-inflammatory properties
  • Mix in some Greek yogurt for protein and probiotics (optional)
  • Almond butter for good healthy fats and satiety (optional)
Read More

item.title: About us
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Just like any other modern family juggling the daily grind: from work, school, activities, and the never-ending quest for a decent night's sleep! And amidst the chaos, we, like many others, found ourselves wanting to prioritize our health and well-being without sacrificing precious time or sanity.

Our Story

The journey to creating Self Health Living began with a life-changing realization when my parents faced significant health challenges during their golden years. The time they set aside to finally travel and take it easy changed their life plans as my mother suffered a minor stroke. She ended up being diagnosed with a carotid artery disease that was exacerbated due to a family history of diabetes and high cholesterol. My father’s health gradually declined as he had both his knees replaced and later went through a heart valve replacement due to a congenital heart disease and eventually needed a pacemaker. Witnessing their struggles and the impact of these conditions on their quality of life was a wake-up call for me. It was a stark reminder of how crucial it is to prioritize health and wellness, not just for ourselves but for the whole family.

Inspired by my family’s resilience and the desire to prevent similar issues in my own life and others, I embarked on a mission to learn and share as much as possible about living a healthier, more balanced life. That’s how Self Health Living was born. We didn’t want to be just another health website telling you what to do. We wanted to be a resource for families who, like us, crave practical solutions for cleaner eating in a busy world.

Welcome to Self Health Living, your go-to resource for living a healthier, more balanced life. Our mission is to empower individuals and families to prioritize their health and well-being through proper nutrition, regular exercise, mindfulness practices, and smart supplementation. We understand the challenges of maintaining a healthy lifestyle, especially for those juggling family responsibilities and that 9-5. That’s why we’re here to provide practical tips, inspiration, and support to help you achieve your wellness goals.

Here's what you can expect from Self Health Living:

  • Real-life, family-friendly recipes: We prioritize nutritious meals that are quick, easy, and actually taste good. No fancy ingredients or complicated techniques, just delicious recipes that fit seamlessly into your busy schedule.
  • Practical tips and hacks to get moving: Regular physical activity is crucial for maintaining a healthy body and mind. Whether you’re a fitness enthusiast or just starting out, we provide guidance on different forms of exercise that can fit into your lifestyle. From quick at-home workouts to outdoor activities, our goal is to help you find enjoyable ways to stay active and boost your energy levels.
  • Meditation and Mindfulness: Mental health is just as important as physical health. We emphasize the benefits of meditation and mindfulness practices to reduce stress, improve focus, and enhance overall well-being. Our blog features beginner-friendly meditation techniques, stress management tips, and ways to incorporate mindfulness into your daily routine.
  • Smart Nutrition: For those with busy lifestyles, getting all the necessary nutrients from diet alone can be challenging. We provide evidence-based information on supplements that can support your health, but only after all natural methods from diet have been exhausted.
  • Honest and relatable stories: We're not perfect. We have picky eaters, busy schedules, and cravings just like you do. But we're also committed to learning and growing together.

Our Commitment

We believe that everyone can be their own Self Health Living, making informed choices that lead to a happier, healthier life. Join us as we explore the world of self health via nutrition, mindfulness and an active lifestyle, one helpful tip at a time!

Join Our Community

At Self Health Living, we believe in the power of community. We invite you to join our growing community of health-conscious individuals who are dedicated to living their best lives. Follow us on social media, subscribe to our newsletter, and participate in our online forums to share your experiences and support others on their wellness journey.

item.title: Mindfulness
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
In our busy, fast-paced world, finding a moment to pause and center ourselves is more important than ever. Many may think of meditation for yogis in saffron robes sitting on top of the Himalaya mountains, but it can also be a mom taking ten minutes in the morning for herself before her day begins. Meditation has become a popular tool for achieving mental clarity, reducing stress, and improving overall well-being. But with so many forms of meditation out there, it can be hard to know where to begin and what's best for you.

In this post, we'll explore the benefits of meditation, highlight different forms of the practice, and give you easy steps to get started with a few forms that we at SelfHealthLiving have found helpful in our lives.

"The quieter you become, the more you can hear."

Ram Dass

"The mind is everything. What you think you become."

Buddha

"The goal of meditation isn't to control your thoughts, it's to stop letting them control you."

Unknown


The Benefits of Meditation

Meditation offers a wide range of benefits for both the mind and body. Studies have shown that regular meditation can:

  1. Reduce stress and anxiety: By calming the mind, meditation helps manage stress levels and reduce the physical symptoms of anxiety. Being able to reduce your stress and anxiety has shown the ability to strengthen your immune system and make you healthier.
  2. Improve Focus and Attention: Meditation trains your brain to focus better, improving productivity and mental sharpness. In this busy world we live in, taking a few minutes to just recenter yourself will do wonders for whatever task you are trying to complete.
  3. Enhance Emotional Health: Mindfulness practices such as simple breath work in between meetings and or classes can increase self-awareness and help regulate emotions, leading to greater emotional resilience not only for that day, if regularly practiced via a lifetime.
  4. Promote Better Sleep: A calm, relaxed mind often leads to better sleep quality is not a new concept. Taking time before bed to relax your mind with a meditation exercise has shown to decrease stress receptors, increase melatonin and slow your heart rate.
  5. Boost Physical Health: Research links meditation to improved heart health, reduced cognitive decline and even strengthened immunity. With regular practice studies have shown meditation can lower blood pressure which over time can reduce the strain on your heart and blood vessels.

Different Types of Meditation

There are many forms of meditation, each with its unique focus and method. Let’s look at a few key types and how to begin practicing them:

1. Mindfulness Meditation

Mindfulness meditation encourages you to focus on the present moment without judgment. It’s about becoming aware of your thoughts, feelings, and sensations, observing them without reacting. This involves accepting what you are thinking about at the moment and being aware without judgment. Be present and aware of what you are experiencing through all your senses. This is great practice to begin on your commute if you are taking mass transit or in between meetings.

How to get started:

  • Find a quiet place where you won’t be disturbed.
  • Sit comfortably and close your eyes.
  • Focus on your breath, noticing the inhale and exhale. When your mind wanders, gently bring your attention back to your breath. Don’t fight your mind wandering, in the beginning this will happen regularly and let it, practice observing them without judgment. As you keep practicing you will see you are more focused on your breath for longer periods of time.
  • Start with just 5-10 minutes a day and gradually increase your time, i find getting small breaks throughout the day helps me center my focus and gives me a much needed mental break.

2. Loving-Kindness Meditation (Metta)

This Buddhist practice focuses on cultivating feelings of compassion, love, and kindness, both for yourself and others. It’s a wonderful way to promote positive emotions and reduce negative ones like anger or resentment. Sometimes I like to think of this meditation as a grateful practice. By taking the time to highlight all that you are grateful for, it allows you to embrace that happiness in your life that you wish to share with others.

How to get started:

  • Sit comfortably, this could be on a chair or criss crossed on the floor and close your eyes.
  • Repeat phrases like, “May I be happy. May I be healthy. May I be safe.” I find taking a moment to think about a time in your life that you were so happy, the moment that you had with a loved one, in college with your friends or when your child was born. Take that moment and soak it in, feel how happy you were and it’s ok to just sit there and breathe while you think of that moment.
  • This practice should be first focused on oneself, but can be expanded to others as you improve your practice. We cannot spread love for others if we don't first learn to unconditionally love ourselves.
  • Then extend those wishes to others, starting with loved ones and expanding to others. At times it may seem artificial, with questions like who is really getting these wishes, but think of loved ones or current events and how love could ne used to solve their problems.   
  • Start with 10 minutes, increasing the time as you feel comfortable.

3. Body Scan Meditation

Body scan meditation helps you connect with your body by focusing on physical sensations. It’s particularly helpful for stress relief and deep relaxation. It is one of the best types of meditation people can try when trying to go to sleep.

How to get started:

  • Lie down in a comfortable position.
  • Close your eyes and begin by focusing on your toes, slowly moving your attention up through your body, part by part. If you lose your concentration, it’s quite alright, be kind to yourself and try again. This is very normal and you will get better with practice.
  • Notice any tension or sensations, and allow your body to relax with each exhale.
  • Practice this for 10-20 minutes to release stress or try it before sleeping to try to improve your sleep hygiene.

3. Transcendental Meditation (TM)

TM is a popular form of meditation where you silently repeat a mantra (a specific word or sound) to help the mind settle into a deep state of relaxation. Originally founded by an Indian Yogi, Maharishi Mahesh Yogi, he wanted practitioners to achieve a fourth major state of consciousness. This practice can be tried just about anywhere, from your bedroom to a doctor’s waiting room. It is the most versatile and for some the easiest to stick to given you typically try to stay focused on your mantra.

How to get started:

  • Choose a mantra that has personal meaning or consult a certified TM teacher. The most common one you will hear is the Om-Aum, "Aum Namah Shivaya", "I am that I am", find one that works best for you. 
  • Sit in a comfortable position that works for you, close your eyes, and start by just breathing in and out a few times until you move on to your mantra, repeat the mantra silently in your mind.
  • Practice twice a day for 20 minutes, as with other mediations give yourself some time. It is ok if your mind wanders and starts thinking about something else, just bring it back to the mantra and train your mind to focus over time.

Tips for a Successful Meditation Practice

01

Start Small:

Don’t feel like you need to meditate for 30 minutes right off the bat. Even 5-10 minutes a day can make a difference. Once you get comfortable

02

Be Consistent:

Try to meditate at the same time each day, building a routine that works for your lifestyle will make you consistent and successful

03

Be Patient With Yourself:

Your mind will wander, especially when you’re starting out. This is completely normal—just gently bring your focus back and try again.

04

Explore Guided Meditations:

Apps like Headspace and Calm offer great guided meditations that can help you ease into the practice. They give you guidance on how best to get started and they are typically timed so if you don’t yet have your internal clock trained it can be useful.

item.title: Move That Body
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Exercise. It's a word that can elicit groans or cheers, depending on who you ask. But one thing is undeniable: regular physical activity is a fundamental pillar of good health. It's not just about looking good (although that's a perk!), it's about feeling your best and giving your body the tools it needs to thrive. We all hear it all the time, movement is the key to a healthy and joyful life. It will provide you with the means to a stronger you, it will help prevent or at least prolong diseases from diabetes to heart disease, but then why is it so hard for so many of us to take the steps to start if not continue making it a part of your life.

Ready to get moving? The good news is, you don't need to become a gym rat. Even moderate-intensity exercise for 30 minutes most days of the week can make a significant difference. Find activities you enjoy, whether it's brisk walking, dancing, swimming, or cycling. Consistency is key, so make physical activity a regular part of your life and experience the transformative power of exercise!

Why is exercise so important? Here's a breakdown of the magic it works on your mind and body:

01

Stronger and Healthier You

Studies have shown regular exercise not only extends your life but drastically improves the quality of life.

02

Enhanced Energy

Feeling sluggish? Exercise is a natural energizer! It improves oxygen delivery to your cells, boosts mitochondrial function (your cellular power plants!), and elevates mood-boosting endorphins. Say goodbye to fatigue and hello to a vibrant, energized you.

03

Sharper Focus

Even a simple walk after lunch will increase blood flow to the brain, enhance cognitive function and improve ability to concentrate, and even help slow down age-related cognitive decline. So get those neurons firing!

04

Disease Prevention Champion

Movement will regulate your blood sugars, strengthen your muscles and improve your immunity with an increase of white blood cells and reduce inflammation.

05

Stress Buster

Regular movement reduces cortisol the stress hormone and releases endorphins that elevate your mood to a feel good state. We can all use some feel good vibes.

06

Sleep Like A Baby

Movement has shown improvement in quality and inducing deeper more restorative sleep.

Staying Active

Strength Training

As we age the importance of strength training magnifies for both men and woman. Keeping muscle strength will allow you to perform the daily activities like carrying groceries or playing with your grandkids.

2 - 3 times Weekly

Cardio

Maintaining a strong vo2 max is one of the leading measurements of extending your lifespan.

2 times Weekly

High Intensity Interval Training (HIIT)

Quick bursts of high intensity training can keep you burning calories almost a much as a light jog for 40-60 mins.

1 - 2 times a week

Yoga and Mind Body Exercises

Flexibility and mental health is the corner stone of maintaining healthy stress levels.

5 - 6 times Weekly

Stretch or Rest Day

We don't build muscle if we don't let our body rest, we need to let our bodies relax to really articulate the hard work you put in.

1 - 2 times Weekly

Have Fun

Play with your kids, go for that golf game with your buddies, not only is it keeping you moving, but building those connections that has been linked to a longer and more enjoyable lifespan.

Every day

item.title: Smoothies
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
The Power of a Balanced Smoothie: A Delicious Way to Boost Your Health

I am a strong believer in incorporating as many vegetables and fruits into my diet as possible. They are a fantastic way to increase your fiber intake, which is essential for digestive health and overall well-being. However, I also emphasize the importance of including adequate protein and healthy fats in my diet to ensure I stay satiated and energized throughout the day. 

One of the best methods I've found to achieve this balance is by starting my day with a smoothie. Smoothies are incredibly versatile and can be tailored to meet your nutritional needs and taste preferences. I typically enjoy a smoothie as a post-workout snack to help with muscle recovery, but it's also a fantastic option for a quick and nutritious breakfast when you're rushing out the door.

The key to making smoothies a consistent part of my diet is ensuring they taste delicious. It's easy to be tempted to add tons of sugar or choose only super sweet fruits to enhance the flavor, but this can negate many of the health benefits. Instead, I've experimented and developed a range of smoothie recipes that are not only nutritious but also taste great. These recipes have been tested and approved by my entire family, making them a staple in our household.

Smoothie Recipes

Gut and Glow Power Smoothie: Drink your probiotics

Mango Carrot Dream: Boost your skin and immunity

Berry Bean Protein Smoothie

Why Smoothies Work

Smoothies offer a convenient way to pack a variety of nutrients into a single meal. By blending fruits, vegetables, protein sources, and healthy fats, I can create a balanced and satisfying drink that supports my health goals. Here’s why I love incorporating smoothies into my routine:

Fiber Boost:

 Fruits and vegetables are rich in dietary fiber, which helps keep my digestive system running smoothly and keeps me feeling full longer.

Protein Power:

Adding a scoop of protein powder, Greek yogurt, or nut butter ensures I get the necessary protein to support muscle repair and growth, especially important after a workout. The added probiotics and additional protein from the Greek yogurt is just an added bonus.

Protein Powder

Greek Yogurt

Healthy Fats:

Including ingredients like avocado, almond butter, chia seeds, or flaxseeds adds healthy fats to my smoothies. These fats are crucial for brain health, hormone production, and keeping me full and satisfied.

The Challenge of Flavor

Let's face it, the easiest way to make a smoothie taste good is to load it with sugar or rely heavily on sweet fruits. While this might make for a delicious treat, it often comes at the cost of spiking your blood sugar levels and reducing the overall nutritional value. Instead, I focus on finding the right balance of flavors using natural ingredients. Here are some tips I use to create delicious and healthy smoothies:

Use naturally sweet fruits: Instead of adding sugar, I use fruits like apples, and various berries, which provide natural sweetness along with essential vitamins and minerals. I will sometimes use tropical fruit like bananas, mangoes and pineapples but keep it to once a week as a treat. Tropical fruit can typically have an abundance of glucose and create a sugar spike.

Incorporating smoothies into your daily routine is a delicious and convenient way to boost your intake of fruits, vegetables, protein, and healthy fats. By experimenting with different ingredients and flavors, you can create nutritious powerhouse drinks that you'll look forward to each day. Remember, the key to making smoothies a consistent part of your diet is to ensure they taste great without relying on added sugars. Try the recipes I've shared and see how they can help you achieve your health goals.

Add Some Spice

Ingredients like ginger, cinnamon, or turmeric can add a unique flavor and additional health benefits. Start with lite doses as they flavor can be over powering.

Pick Your Greens

Spinach and kale are mild in flavor but packed with nutrients. They blend seamlessly into smoothies, adding a nutritional boost without overwhelming the taste.

Balance with Protein and Fats

Including a protein source which easily can be added with protein powders and healthy fats helps create a satisfying and well-rounded smoothie that keeps you full longer.

item.title: Lunch
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Oh, the midday slump! The post-lunch "food coma" that has us battling yawns at our desks and questioning all our life choices. We've all been there. You grab something quick, something convenient, something that smells delicious in the moment... and then BAM! An hour later, you're fighting the urge to nap under your desk, wondering where all your energy went.

Sound familiar? You're not alone! Lunch, while crucial for fueling our afternoon, often feels like a battlefield where convenience and health duke it out. We're rushing, we're stressed, and finding something genuinely healthy, quick, and not a carb overload seems like a mythical quest.

But what if I told you it doesn't have to be? What if you could have a lunch that leaves you energized, focused, and ready to conquer your afternoon, instead of craving a caffeine IV?

The Midday Meal Dilemma: Why Lunch Matters (and Why It's So Hard!)

Lunch isn't just a break; it's a vital pit stop that impacts your energy, mood, and productivity for the rest of the day. Skipping it or fueling up on the wrong stuff can lead to:

  • The Dreaded Food Coma: That heavy, sluggish feeling. It's often caused by a rapid spike in blood sugar from high-carb, low-protein meals, followed by a quick crash. Your body goes into overdrive processing the carbs, leaving you drained.
  • Energy Rollercoaster: Mood swings, irritability, and difficulty concentrating are common culprits of unstable blood sugar after an unbalanced lunch.
  • Unhealthy Defaults: When time is tight, the drive-thru, the ultra-processed ready meal, or that giant bagel often become the default. They're quick, but they rarely serve your health goals.
  • Decision Fatigue: Just figuring out what to eat can feel like an insurmountable task when you're already juggling a million things.

The good news? It doesn't have to be this way! The secret to a successful, carb-coma-free lunch lies in prioritizing protein and healthy fats, while being mindful of your carbohydrate choices. This combo provides sustained energy, keeps you feeling full, and helps stabilize blood sugar.

Conquering the Carb Coma: Your Healthy, High-Protein, Low-Carb Lunch Arsenal!

I'm all about making healthy eating enjoyable and easy. So, here are some fantastic, quick, and flavorful lunch options from our very own recipe archives that will keep you energized and away from that afternoon slump:

Lunch Highlights

Dive into these standout meals, perfect for those looking to enjoy something truly special.

Chicken Caesar Wrap

Grilled chicken, tomatoes, lettuce, avocado and parmesan cheese

Chicken and Black Bean Tostadas

Smokey Grilled Chicken, garlic, crushed black pepper, red onions, tomatoes, avocado

Chipotle Chicken Salad Bowl

Smoky Chicken breast with adobo peppers, over fresh greens that will power your day.

item.title: Breakfast
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Breakfast might just be the most debated meal of the day! On one side, we’ve got the intermittent fasters, who believe skipping breakfast gives your digestive system a much-needed break. On the other side are breakfast champions who swear it’s the ultimate energy boost and key to preventing midday overeating.

So, who’s right? Research shows both can work—as long as you’re fueling your body with the right foods. Personally, I believe the real focus should be on quality meals that truly nourish us. After all, whether you’re an early-morning eater or a breakfast skipper, what really counts is making each meal count!

Eggs with Prosciutto and Avocado on a Almond Tortilla

Chickpea Pancake

Savory Smoked Salmon Avocado Toast

item.title: Dinner
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Why Dinner Matters: Healthy, Quick, and Meaningful Meals to End Your Day Right

Discover why dinner is more than just a meal. Learn how to make it nourishing, simple, and a time to reconnect—with quick and healthy dinner recipes from SelfHealth Living.

Dinner: The Most Underrated Health Ritual You’re Probably Rushing Through

Ah, dinner. The finish line. The one meal that should feel like a deep exhale after a long day…

And yet, how often does it end up being a microwave scramble, a random fridge forage, or a last-minute “let’s just order in”?

We’ve all been there. You’re tired, you’re hungry, and the idea of chopping anything feels like the last thing you want to do. So you default to what’s quick — or worse, skip it altogether, order out or end up snacking your way through until full.

But here’s the thing: dinner is kind of a big deal — nutritionally and emotionally.

Why Dinner Deserves More Respect (And Why We Often Blow It Off)

Dinner is more than just “food at the end of the day.” It’s your body’s final fuel source before a long overnight fast, and for many of us, it’s the most complete, nutrient-rich meal we eat.

But let’s be honest — it’s also the meal most likely to fall apart because:

  • We’re exhausted. After a long day, our willpower’s at zero, and the couch is calling.
  • We skip the plan. No dinner plan = random outcomes. Like cereal. Or chips and hummus. Again.
  • We go too heavy or too light. Either we overload on comfort carbs or barely eat, setting ourselves up for late-night snacking.
  • It becomes background noise. Eating in front of a screen or multitasking — dinner becomes an afterthought instead of a reset.

Reclaiming the Table: Why Dinner Should Matter

Dinner is your chance to:

  • Refuel right. Balanced meals support digestion, sleep, and muscle recovery.
  • Create calm. A screen-free 20-minute meal can anchor your entire evening.
  • Reconnect. Whether it’s family, friends, or solo mindfulness — shared meals build presence.

At SelfHealthLiving, our goal is to help you end the day strong — with meals that are nourishing, satisfying, and actually doable.

Healthy Chickpea Curry

Smoked Salmon Power Bowl

Miso Glazed Cod

item.title: Nutrition
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
We at Self Health Living strive to bring to you amazing nutritional facts because we feel it is the cornerstone of good health, influencing everything from energy levels and immune function to mental clarity and emotional well-being. We understand that maintaining a balanced diet can be challenging amidst busy schedules and family commitments. That’s why we’re dedicated to providing practical, easy-to-follow advice and delicious recipes that make healthy eating accessible and enjoyable. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge and tools needed to nourish your body effectively, supporting long-term wellness and a vibrant, active life.

We can't and shouldn't resort to supplements to meet our daily nutritional needs, but when I am asked what are the primary areas I should focus on, the list below is what always comes to mind. Each nutrient and or supplement is vital and we here feel will give you the biggest impact on your health. Let's dive deeper into each and discover why this nutrient is so vital to making us stronger, wiser and just feeling great.

01

Protein

Protein is like the body's building blocks: it repairs and builds tissues, keeping you healthy.

02

Fiber

Fiber acts like your gut's superhero, it helps digestion, maintains a protective biome and reduces disease risk.

03

Magnesium

Usually an under appreciated mineral that plays a critical role in our bodies from sleep, strong bones and efficient nerve processing.

04

Ashwagandha

An herb with a long history of use in Ayurvedic medicine that helps with stress, anxiety reduction and sleep aid.

05

Vitamin D

An abundantly researched nutrient that helps with our bones, muscle function, immune and brain health.

06

Collagen

The most abundant protein in your body, providing support for your skin, bones, muscles, and connective tissues.

item.title: Recipes
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Eating well is one of the best ways to maintain your health, energy, and overall well-being. Whether you're kickstarting your morning with a hearty breakfast, enjoying a refreshing lunch, recharging with an afternoon snack, or winding down with a satisfying dinner, each meal plays a crucial role in your daily routine.

In this series, we'll explore a variety of great recipes for all meals of the day. From nutrient-packed smoothies and energizing breakfasts to light yet fulfilling lunches, and from wholesome snacks to delectable dinners, we've got you covered. Our recipes are designed with busy individuals and families in mind, ensuring that you can enjoy flavorful, healthy meals without spending hours in the kitchen.

Join us on this culinary journey as we share delicious, easy-to-follow recipes that will help you and your loved ones stay nourished and energized throughout the day. Let’s make every meal an opportunity to fuel your body and delight your taste buds!

Smoothie Recipes

Smoothies are a quick way to get in your fiber and protein first thing in the morning or when you are craving a snack.

Breakfast Recipes

Breakfast is a way to start your day right, we explore delicious recipes that are quick and nutritious.

Lunch Recipes

Lunch is so important, for some it is the first meal of the day and making it right is going to avoid the mid afternoon crash.

Dinner Recipes

Dinner is for the ones you love, it's the time to replenish your energy, connect and relax. Let's make some dinner the family will look forward to.

item.title: Self Health Insights
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to the official SelfHealthLiving blog! Here you'll find our newest articles covering everything you need to improve your overall wellbeing. Dive into strategies for eating right, effective at-home yoga and meditation routines, and actionable tips for getting moving daily. Start your journey to a healthier, more active life today.

4 min read

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Privacy Policy

Effective Date: May 9, 2026 Self Health Living ("we", "us", or "our") operates the SelfHealth iOS application (the "App"). This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal information. By using the App, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this policy.

1. Information Collection & Usage

To provide a personalized health experience, we collect data in the following ways:
  • Account Data: Email and password used for authentication and cross-device syncing via Supabase.
  • User-Entered Health Data: Lab result values (e.g., cholesterol, glucose), cycle tracking entries, and personal health notes.
  • HealthKit Integration: With your permission, we read metrics like heart rate, HRV, and sleep. This data is processed locally to generate health scores and shared with AI providers only when you request specific coaching insights.
    • HealthKit data is never used for advertising or shared with third parties for purposes unrelated to health and fitness.
  • Usage Data:App interaction logs (e.g., screens visited, errors) collected anonymously for debugging and improvement.

2. Artificial Intelligence (AI) Features

SelfHealth utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) to provide automated health coaching and lab analysis.

  • Providers: We currently use Google Gemini (operated by Google LLC) and Anthropic Claude. The AI provider we use may change over time and this policy will be updated to reflect any such changes.
  • What data is sent: When you use an AI feature, the following data may be transmitted to the AI provider: your health metrics and scores (e.g., HRV, sleep, vitals score), lab result values, biomarker data, workout and exercise history (e.g., exercises performed, weights, repetitions, and dates) and recovery indicators, and the text of your query. Raw HealthKit sensor data is never sent.
  • Your consent: We will always ask for your explicit permission before sending any personal health data to a third-party AI service. You can review or revoke this consent at any time via Settings → AI & Data Usage.
  • Security: Data is sent via encrypted Enterprise APIs. Your data is never used to train the public models of these providers.
  • Retention: Personal data sent to AI providers is typically deleted within 30 days.
  • Third-party privacy policies: Google Privacy Policy · Anthropic Privacy Policy
You may opt out of AI features at any time via Settings → AI & Data Usage, though this will limit personalized coaching functionality.

3. Medical Disclaimer

Important: All App content, including AI-generated coaching and lab analysis, is for informational purposes only. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

4. Storage, Security & Deletion

  • Infrastructure: Data is stored securely in Supabase with Row Level Security (RLS) and end-to-end encryption.
  • Data Control: You can delete your account at any time via Settings → Delete Account. This permanently and irreversibly wipes all your data from our servers.
  • Third-Party Sales: We never sell your health data to advertisers or third-party data brokers.

5. Contact Us

Questions regarding your privacy? Reach out at: Self Health Living Email: privacy@selfhealthliving.com Web: selfhealthliving.com

item.title: AI & Your Blood Work: Personalized Longevity Insights for a Healthier You
item.dc:creator: health_agent
item.content:encoded:
Have you ever looked at your annual blood test results, seen everything in the 'normal' range, and thought, 'Is that really the whole story?' For many of us, especially as we navigate our 40s and beyond, 'normal' isn't always 'optimal.' A recent WSJ article highlighted a growing trend: people uploading their blood work to AI platforms to gain deeper, more personalized insights into their health. Is this oversharing, or is it a smart step towards owning our health journey?

Beyond the Basics: Why Your Blood Work is a Goldmine

Your blood work is a rich tapestry of information, far more than just a snapshot of your cholesterol or blood sugar. It's a dynamic report on your metabolic health, inflammation levels, hormonal balance, and even nutrient status. For those of us focused on longevity and vitality, understanding these markers is crucial.

Traditional blood test results are often presented with broad 'normal' ranges, based on population averages. But what's average isn't necessarily what's ideal for you. As we age, subtle shifts in these markers can signal potential issues long before they manifest as chronic conditions. Recognizing these nuances is key to proactive health management.

AI: Your New Health Detective

This is where AI steps in as a powerful new ally. Instead of simply flagging values outside a generic range, AI algorithms can analyze complex patterns across dozens of blood markers. They can identify subtle trends, correlations, and potential imbalances that a human eye might miss in a quick review. Imagine an AI looking at your inflammatory markers, your insulin sensitivity, and your nutrient levels, then cross-referencing them with a vast database of optimal health data and research.

The beauty of AI-driven analysis lies in its ability to offer truly personalized insights. It can help you understand:

  • Which specific dietary adjustments might lower your inflammation.
  • What type of exercise could best support your metabolic health.
  • If certain nutrient deficiencies are impacting your energy or cognitive function.

This isn't pseudo-science; it's data science applied to biology. It empowers you to move from general health advice to highly targeted, evidence-informed strategies designed for your unique physiology – a game-changer for extending your healthspan.

Navigating the Future of Personalized Health

While the prospect of AI-driven health insights is exciting, it's essential to approach it with a balanced perspective. Data privacy and the reputation of the platforms you choose are paramount. Always remember that AI is a tool to augment, not replace, the expertise of your healthcare provider. Think of it as an advanced assistant that helps you gather more information to have richer, more informed conversations with your doctor.

Here are your actionable steps to leverage this new frontier for optimal longevity:

  • Get Comprehensive Blood Work Regularly: Don't settle for the bare minimum. Ask your doctor for comprehensive panels that include markers like hs-CRP (inflammation), homocysteine, HbA1c, Vitamin D, full thyroid panels, and a complete hormone profile. The more data, the better the AI's analysis.
  • Explore Vetted AI Health Platforms: Research reputable platforms that specialize in personalized health data analysis. Look for those with strong data security policies, clear disclaimers that their insights are not medical advice, and a transparent approach to their algorithms. Start with free or low-cost trials to see if the insights resonate.
  • Collaborate with Your Doctor: Bring the insights generated by AI to your physician. Use them as a starting point for discussion, further testing, and to collaboratively develop a personalized health plan. Your doctor's medical expertise, combined with AI-driven data, creates a powerful synergy for your health.

Embracing AI with your blood work isn't oversharing; it's a proactive step towards a more informed, optimized, and vibrant future. It's about taking control of your health narrative and designing a longevity plan that's uniquely yours.

Source: Read the original article
item.excerpt:encoded: Unlock personalized health insights with AI analysis of your blood work. Discover how this new tech empowers adults 40+ for optimal longevity and vitality.

item.title: Support
item.dc:creator: health_agent
item.content:encoded:
Everything you need to get the most out of SelfHealth Living — from connecting Apple Health to understanding your bloodwork and managing your subscription.

↓ HealthKit Sync ↓ Troubleshooting ↓ Go Pro ↓ Lab Import ↓ Storage & Backup
HealthKit & Data Sync
Syncing with Apple Health

SelfHealth reads your data directly from Apple Health. On first launch you'll see a permissions prompt — tap Allow All to give the app access to everything it needs.

To review or fix permissions later:

  1. Open the iPhone Settings app
  2. Tap Privacy & Security → Health
  3. Tap SelfHealth Living
  4. Turn on all categories — especially Heart, Activity, Sleep, Nutrition, and Body Measurements
SelfHealth never writes to Apple Health. It only reads data that other apps and your Apple Watch have already logged there.

Any device or app that syncs to Apple Health automatically appears in SelfHealth:

  • Apple Watch — heart rate, HRV, VO₂ max, sleep, workouts, ECG
  • Oura Ring — sleep stages, readiness, body temperature
  • Whoop — strain, recovery, sleep
  • Garmin, Fitbit, Polar — workouts and activity via their companion apps
  • MyFitnessPal, Cronometer, Lose It — nutrition and calories
  • Withings, Renpho — weight and body composition

Enable Apple Health sync inside each device's companion app and the data flows into SelfHealth automatically.

This is almost always a permissions issue. Work through these steps:

  1. Check HealthKit permissions — make sure all categories are turned on (see above)
  2. Force-close SelfHealth (swipe up from the app switcher) and reopen it
  3. Open the Health app and confirm your data is present in the Browse tab
  4. If using Oura or Whoop, open their app and force a sync first
The dashboard shows today's data. If you just installed the app mid-day, metrics like HRV and sleep score only appear after your next full night's sleep is recorded.

Heart metrics & vitals score

SelfHealth tracks your heart rate, HRV, VO₂ max, resting heart rate, and overall vitals score in one place — pulling data directly from your Apple Watch or compatible wearable via Apple Health.

These metrics require an Apple Watch or compatible wearable.

  • HRV — recorded automatically during sleep by Apple Watch (watchOS 7+). Won't appear if you didn't wear your watch overnight.
  • VO₂ max — estimated during outdoor walks, runs, or hikes (10+ minutes with GPS).
  • Resting heart rate — takes 3–5 days after first setup to appear.

If the data is in the Apple Health app but not in SelfHealth, double-check HealthKit permissions for the Heart category.

Troubleshooting
Common Problems
  1. Force-close the app and reopen it
  2. Restart your iPhone
  3. Check the App Store for a SelfHealth update
  4. If the problem persists, go to Settings → Reset App Data inside SelfHealth — this won't affect your Apple Health data
  1. Check your internet connection
  2. Tap Forgot Password to reset via email
  3. If you signed up with Apple, tap Sign in with Apple — use the same Apple ID you registered with
  4. Still stuck? Email support@selfhealthliving.com with your account email
  1. Go to iPhone Settings → Notifications → SelfHealth Living and confirm notifications are enabled
  2. Check that Focus / Do Not Disturb isn't silencing the app
  3. Inside SelfHealth, go to Settings and confirm reminders are turned on

SelfHealth uses AI to parse lab PDFs but formats vary by lab. Here's what helps:

  • Use the original PDF from your lab's patient portal — scanned photos are less accurate
  • Make sure the PDF is not password-protected
  • If a marker is missing, tap + Add Marker on the Bloodwork screen to enter it manually
Email us your lab name if imports consistently fail — we'll add support for it.
Pro Subscription
Going Pro
  • Unlimited AI health assistant queries
  • Full bloodwork history, trend analysis, and AI interpretation
  • Advanced health score breakdown with weekly trends
  • Detailed protocol and supplement tracking
  • Coach commands and personalised health insights
  • Cloud backup and sync across devices

The free tier lets you view your dashboard and basic metrics with no time limit.

  1. Tap any Pro-locked feature inside SelfHealth, or go to Settings → Upgrade to Pro
  2. Choose Monthly ($9.99/month) or Annual ($99.99/year — best value)
  3. Confirm with Face ID / Touch ID

Billing is handled by Apple. Cancel any time from iPhone Settings → [Your Name] → Subscriptions.

Go to Settings → Restore Purchases inside SelfHealth. This re-checks your subscription with Apple and unlocks Pro immediately.

Restore Purchases only works if you're signed in with the Apple ID that made the original purchase.
  1. Open iPhone Settings → [Your Name] → Subscriptions
  2. Tap SelfHealth Living
  3. Tap Cancel Subscription

You keep Pro access until the end of your current billing period.

Lab Results
Importing Your Bloodwork

See every marker in context

SelfHealth parses your lab PDF and maps each result against optimal ranges — not just the lab's reference range. Markers are grouped by category (Cardiovascular, Metabolic, Hormones, etc.) and colour-coded so you can spot what needs attention at a glance.

The fastest method is importing a PDF directly from your lab's patient portal:

  1. Log into Quest MyQuest, LabCorp Patient, or your lab's portal on your phone
  2. Download your results as a PDF
  3. In SelfHealth, tap Bloodwork → Import → PDF
  4. Select the file from Files or your Downloads folder
  5. Review the AI-parsed markers and confirm
PDFs from lab portals are far more accurate than photos. If you only have a paper copy, use good lighting and keep the camera parallel to the page.
  • Quest Diagnostics
  • LabCorp
  • BioReference
  • Sonora Quest
  • Cleveland HeartLab
  • Any lab that exports a standard PDF with marker name, value, unit, and reference range

Try importing even if your lab isn't listed. If it consistently fails, email us the lab name and we'll add support.

Yes — on the Bloodwork screen tap + → Add Manually. Enter the marker name, value, unit, and test date. Useful for individual markers missed by the PDF parser or printed reports.

Tap any marker to open its detail view, then tap Ask AI. You can also open the Health Assistant (the ✦ button on the dashboard) and ask "What do my latest cholesterol results mean?" for a full analysis.

SelfHealth's AI is educational only. Always discuss your lab results with your doctor before making health decisions.

Meet Aliya, your AI health assistant

Ask Aliya anything about your health data — from interpreting lab results to understanding your sleep trends. Tap the ✦ button on the dashboard to open the assistant at any time.

Privacy & Storage
On-Device vs Cloud Backup

All your SelfHealth data — bloodwork, notes, supplements, health scores — stays only on your iPhone. Nothing is sent to our servers.

Pros: maximum privacy, works offline, no account required.
Cons: if you lose your phone without a backup, SelfHealth data is gone; no cross-device sync.

On-Device data is included in your iCloud Backup. As long as iPhone backups are on, your data is safe when switching phones.

Your SelfHealth data syncs securely to our servers (encrypted in transit and at rest). Requires a SelfHealth account.

Pros: safe even if your phone is lost; restore everything by signing in on a new device.
Cons: requires internet to sync; data lives on our servers — see our Privacy Policy.

  • Privacy first + regular iPhone backups → choose On-Device
  • Peace of mind + plan to switch phones → choose Cloud Backup

Change it any time in SelfHealth → Settings → Data Storage. Switching to Cloud uploads your existing on-device data; switching to On-Device stops syncing but doesn't delete server data.

Yes. All data in transit uses TLS encryption. Data on our servers is encrypted at rest. We never sell your data or share it for advertising. Full details in our Privacy Policy.

Platform & Availability
Supported Devices

SelfHealth Living is currently available on iPhone only. An Android version is actively in development and we're working hard to bring it to you.

Sign up for our newsletter to be the first to know when Android launches and stay up to date on all new features.

Join the newsletter at selfhealthliving.com/app to get notified the moment Android is available.

Still need help?

Can't find what you're looking for? We typically reply within one business day.

Email Support

item.title: Strawberry Sunrise: Blend for Peak Performance
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Strawberry Sunrise

This smoothie is a heart and gut healthy explosion of flavors, providing a balanced mix of vitamins, minerals, antioxidants, fiber, and probiotics. It's excellent for supporting immune health, enhancing energy levels, and promoting skin rejuvenation and overall wellness.

  • blender
  • 1 cup Strawberries (Frozen or Fresh)
  • 2/3 cup Kale (If you decide to go fresh make it a cup)
  • 4-5 Baby Carrots
  • 1/3 Apple (Sliced)
  • 2 tbsp Greek Yogurt
  • 1 serving Collagen Powder
  • 1 tsp Flaxseed Oil (Can use actual flaxseed, but make sure its fine )
  • 1 cup Water

  1. Step 1 : Add all the ingredients into a blender, I prefer a Vitamix as it is able to blend all ingredients very smoothly and no one really likes chunks in a smoothie.

    Step 2 : Top with sliced strawberries or a sliced apple if you want it to look pretty, but if your throwing it into a shaker bottle, i would just skip this step.

    Step 3 : Enjoy a nutrient dense and probiotic packed beverage you just created.

Strawberry Sunrise Smoothie: A Heart and Gut Healthy Explosion of Flavors

This smoothie is a heart and gut healthy explosion of flavors, providing a balanced mix of vitamins, minerals, antioxidants, fiber, and probiotics. It's excellent for supporting immune health, enhancing energy levels, promoting skin rejuvenation, and overall wellness. Each ingredient contributes unique benefits, making this smoothie a fantastic choice for a nutritious start to your day.

Ingredients and Health Benefits:

  1. 1 Cup of Frozen Strawberries

   - Benefits: Strawberries are rich in antioxidants, particularly vitamin C and manganese. They help boost immune health, improve skin health, and reduce inflammation.

   - Studies: Consumption of strawberries is linked to improved heart health and reduced oxidative stress.

  1. 2/3 Cup Frozen Kale (Frozen kale is more condensed, if using fresh use 1 cup)

   - Benefits: Kale is a nutrient-dense green leafy vegetable packed with vitamins A, C, and K, as well as antioxidants and fiber. It supports eye health, immune function, and helps maintain healthy blood pressure levels.

   - Studies: Kale has been shown to provide cardiovascular benefits and anti-inflammatory effects.

  1. 4-5 Baby Carrots

   - Benefits: Carrots are high in beta-carotene, which the body converts to vitamin A, crucial for good vision, immune function, and skin health. They also provide dietary fiber and several other vitamins and minerals.

   - Studies: Regular consumption of carrots is associated with improved eye health and reduced risk of certain cancers.

  1. ⅓ of an Apple

   - Benefits: Apples are rich in fiber, particularly pectin, which aids in digestion and helps maintain healthy blood sugar levels. They are also a good source of vitamin C and various antioxidants.

   - Studies: Apples have been linked to lower risk of chronic diseases, including cardiovascular disease.

  1. 2 Tablespoons of Greek Yogurt

   - Benefits: Greek yogurt is a great source of protein, probiotics, calcium, and vitamin B12. It supports gut health by promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and aids in digestion.

   - Studies: Probiotics in Greek yogurt have been shown to improve digestive health and enhance immune function.

  1. 1 Serving of Collagen Powder

   - Benefits: Collagen powder (paid link) provides essential amino acids that support skin elasticity, joint health, and muscle repair. It helps improve skin hydration and reduce wrinkles.

   - Studies: Collagen supplementation has been shown to improve skin health and reduce joint pain.

  1. 1 Teaspoon of Flaxseed Oil

   - Benefits: Flaxseed oil is rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which support heart health, reduce inflammation, and promote brain function. It also provides fiber that aids in digestion.

   - Studies: Flaxseed oil consumption is associated with improved cardiovascular health and anti-inflammatory effects.

Recipe Instructions:

  1. Place all the ingredients in a blender.
  2. Add 1 cup of water or almond milk to achieve your desired consistency.
  3. Blend until smooth and creamy.
  4. Pour into a glass and enjoy immediately.

Conclusion

Incorporating this Strawberry Sunrise Smoothie into your diet is a delicious way to boost your intake of essential nutrients. The combination of fruits, vegetables, protein, healthy fats, and probiotics provides a balanced meal that supports heart health, gut health, and overall wellness. Enjoy it as a refreshing breakfast or a nutrient-packed snack to keep you energized and nourished throughout the day.

Breakfast, Smoothies, Snack
Nutricius

item.title: Purple Protein Smoothie: Post Workout Nutrient Packed Powerhouse
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Purple Protein

This smoothie is a powerhouse of nutrients, providing a balanced mix of vitamins, minerals, antioxidants, fiber, and protein. It's excellent for supporting immune health, enhancing energy levels, and promoting muscle recovery and overall wellness.

  • blender
  • 1 Cup Frozen Blueberries
  • 2/3 Cup Frozen Spinach (If using fresh spinach make it a cup)
  • 4-5 Baby Carrots
  • 1/3 Sliced Apple
  • 1/3 Small Beet (Increase the amount depending on your preference. I am usually ok with a full small beet.)
  • 1 tbsp Almond Butter
  • 1 Serving Protein Powder

Purple Protein Smoothie: A Nutrient-Packed Powerhouse

 

The Purple Protein Smoothie is a powerhouse of nutrients, providing a balanced mix of vitamins, minerals, antioxidants, fiber, and protein. It's excellent for supporting immune health, enhancing energy levels, promoting muscle recovery, and overall wellness. Each ingredient brings unique benefits, making this smoothie a fantastic addition to your daily routine.

 

Ingredients and Health Benefits:

 

  1. 1 Cup of Frozen Blueberries

   - Benefits: Blueberries are rich in antioxidants, particularly anthocyanins, which protect cells from oxidative stress and inflammation. They are also high in vitamin C, vitamin K, and fiber.

   - Studies: Research indicates that blueberry consumption is linked to improved cardiovascular health and cognitive function.

 

  1. 2/3 Cup Frozen Spinach (make a full cup if using fresh spinach)

   - Benefits: Spinach is loaded with vitamins A, C, and K, as well as folate, iron, and calcium. It supports eye health, immune function, and helps maintain healthy blood pressure levels.

   - Studies: Spinach is known for its antioxidant properties and potential to reduce oxidative stress.

 

  1. 4-5 Baby Carrots

   - Benefits: Carrots are high in beta-carotene, which the body converts to vitamin A, crucial for good vision, immune function, and skin health. They also provide dietary fiber and several other vitamins and minerals.

   - Studies: Carrots contribute to reduced risk of certain cancers and improved eye health.

 

  1. ⅓ of an Apple

   - Benefits: Apples are rich in fiber, particularly pectin, which aids in digestion and helps maintain healthy blood sugar levels. They are also a good source of vitamin C and various antioxidants.

   - Studies: Regular apple consumption is associated with a lower risk of chronic diseases, including cardiovascular disease.

 

  1. ⅓ of a Small Beet (Pre-Boiled)

   - Benefits: Beets are high in nitrates, which can improve blood flow and lower blood pressure. They are also a good source of fiber, folate, and vitamin C.

   - Studies: Beets have been shown to enhance exercise performance and reduce blood pressure.

 

  1. 1 Tablespoon of Almond Butter

   - Benefits: Almond butter provides healthy fats, protein, fiber, and essential nutrients like vitamin E, magnesium, and potassium. It supports heart health, helps control blood sugar levels, and promotes satiety.

   - Studies: Nuts and nut butters have been linked to a lower risk of cardiovascular disease and improved blood lipid profiles.

 

  1. 1 Serving of Protein Powder

   - Benefits: Protein powder is an excellent source of high-quality protein, which is essential for muscle repair and growth. It helps keep you full and can aid in weight management.

   - Studies: Protein supplementation is beneficial for muscle recovery and overall muscle health, especially post-exercise.

 

Recipe Instructions:

  1. Place all the ingredients in a blender.
  2. Add 1 cup of water or almond milk to achieve your desired consistency.
  3. Blend until smooth and creamy.
  4. Pour into a glass and enjoy immediately.

 

Conclusion

Incorporating this Purple Protein Smoothie into your diet is a delicious way to boost your intake of essential nutrients. The combination of fruits, vegetables, healthy fats, and protein provides a balanced meal that supports your overall health and wellness. Whether you enjoy it as a post-workout snack or a quick breakfast, this smoothie will help you stay energized and nourished throughout the day.

Smoothies

item.title: Berry Supreme: A Fortifying Blend for Peak Performance
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Berry Supreme

One of my favorite smoothie recipes, and my go to. It brings together antioxidants, protein, good fats and fiber, plus it just tastes great!!

  • blender
  • 1 cup Mixed berries (I prefer frozen)
  • 2/3 cup Spinach (Fresh or frozen)
  • 4-5 Baby Carrots
  • 1/3 Apple (Sliced)
  • 1 tbsp Almond Butter (Splurge on the good stuff)
  • 1 serving Protein Powder (Your preference but I prefer Whey)
  • 1 tsp Curcumin (Prefer the extract)
  • 1.5 cups Water (Adjust the water depending on how thick you want it to be)

  1. Step 1 : Add all the ingredients into a blender, I prefer a Vitamix as it is able to blend all ingredients very smoothly and no one really likes chunks in a smoothie.

    Step 2 : Top with berries if you want it to look pretty, but if your throwing it into a shaker bottle, i would just skip this step.

    Step 3 : Enjoy a nutrient dense, protein packed beverage you just created.

The Berry Supreme Smoothie: A Fortifying Blend for Peak Performance

The Berry Supreme Smoothie is a vibrantly colored and nutrient-rich beverage designed to provide a powerful start to your day. This meticulously crafted blend boasts a wealth of antioxidants, fiber, and protein, all working synergistically to support optimal health and sustained energy levels.

  1. 1 Cup of Frozen Mixed Berries

   - Benefits: Mixed berries, such as blueberries, strawberries, and raspberries, are rich in antioxidants, particularly vitamin C and anthocyanins. These antioxidants help combat oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and support overall immune health.

   - Studies: Research shows that regular consumption of berries is associated with improved heart health and cognitive function  .

  1. 2/3 Cup Frozen Spinach

   - Benefits: Spinach is packed with vitamins A, C, and K, as well as folate, iron, and calcium. It supports eye health, boosts immune function, and helps maintain healthy blood pressure levels.

   - Studies: Spinach's high antioxidant content provides cardiovascular benefits and reduces oxidative stress  .

  1. 4-5 Baby Carrots

   - Benefits: Carrots are high in beta-carotene, which the body converts to vitamin A, essential for good vision, immune function, and skin health. They also provide dietary fiber and several other vitamins and minerals.

   - Studies: Carrots contribute to reduced risk of certain cancers and improved eye health  .

  1. ⅓ of an Apple (add more if you enjoy a stronger apple taste)

  - Benefits: Apples are rich in fiber, particularly pectin, which aids in digestion and helps maintain healthy blood sugar levels. They are also a good source of vitamin C and various antioxidants.

   - Studies: Regular apple consumption is associated with a lower risk of chronic diseases, including cardiovascular disease.

  1. 1 Tablespoon of Almond Butter

   - Benefits: Almond butter provides healthy fats, protein, fiber, and essential nutrients like vitamin E, magnesium, and potassium. It supports heart health, helps control blood sugar levels, and promotes satiety.

   - Studies: Nuts and nut butters have been linked to a lower risk of cardiovascular disease and improved blood lipid profiles.

  1. 1 Serving of Protein Powder

   - Benefits: Protein powder is an excellent source of high-quality protein, which is essential for muscle repair and growth. It helps keep you full and can aid in weight management.

   - Studies: Protein supplementation is beneficial for muscle recovery and overall muscle health, especially post-exercise  .

  1. 1 Serving of Curcumin (or 1 Teaspoon of Turmeric)

   - Benefits: Curcumin, the active ingredient in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It supports joint health, enhances brain function, and may help reduce the risk of chronic diseases.

   - Studies: Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, and it has potential benefits for brain health and cardiovascular function  .

Recipe Instructions:

  1. Place all the ingredients in a blender.
  2. Add 1 cup of water or almond milk to achieve your desired consistency.
  3. Blend until smooth and creamy.
  4. Pour into a glass and enjoy immediately.

Conclusion:

Incorporating the Berry Supreme Smoothie into your daily diet is a delicious and convenient way to boost your intake of essential nutrients. The combination of fruits, vegetables, protein, and healthy fats provides a balanced meal that supports sustained energy, immune health, and overall wellness. Enjoy this smoothie as a nutritious breakfast or a refreshing snack to keep you energized throughout the day.
