item.title: Protein: The Body's Building Block:
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Protein is the powerhouse nutrient that has rightfully earned its place in the spotlight. It plays a pivotal role in our daily lives, aiding in the repair and regeneration of cells, which is essential for building strong bones, muscles, and cartilage, as well as revitalizing our skin. Beyond these benefits, protein is crucial for energy production, hormone regulation, and bolstering a robust immune system.

As we age, protein becomes increasingly vital due to the natural loss of muscle mass. In fact, maintaining the muscle you already have often requires strength training. You might wonder, 'Why does this matter? My parents didn't focus on protein, and they seem fine.' While they may appear healthy on the surface, aging brings a gradual decline in strength, balance, and bone density—all crucial areas we need to continue to embrace for aging gracefully.

The loss of muscle makes everyday tasks more challenging, like climbing stairs or carrying groceries. Take what I call the 'sigh test': Notice how older individuals often sigh with relief when they sit down. This can be a sign of simple fatigue, but more often, it's a response to aches and pains caused by weakened muscles. Ensuring adequate protein intake can help counter these effects, making it easier to maintain an active and fulfilling lifestyle.

Key Benefits of Protein:

1. Muscle Maintenance:

Protein helps build and repair muscle tissue, crucial for strength and mobility and also Protein helps counteract age-related muscle loss (sarcopenia).

2. Bone Health:

 Protein works with calcium to support strong bones, reducing the risk of osteoporosis.

3. Energy Production:

Protein provides a steady stream of energy, helping to prevent fatigue.

4. Immune System Support:

Protein is essential for the production of antibodies, which fight off infections.

5. Hormone Regulation:

Protein plays a role in the production of various hormones, including growth hormone and insulin.

6. Weight Management:

Protein can help you feel full and satisfied, reducing the likelihood of overeating and promoting weight management.

7. Skin and Hair Health:

Protein is a component of collagen and elastin, which are essential for healthy skin and hair. Look at most of the supplements for hair and nail, they will usually include collagen which is protein.

How to Incorporate More Protein to Everyday Meals

1

Start Your Day with Protein

  • Protein-Rich Breakfast: Opt for eggs, Greek yogurt, cottage cheese, or a protein smoothie for breakfast. These options provide a high-protein start to your day, keeping you full longer.
  • Add Protein to Oatmeal: Stir in protein powder, Greek yogurt, or a dollop of nut butter or some nuts to your morning oats or just mix it as part of your overnight oats.
  • Get Creative: Try making a savory pancake with chickpea flour. Indians have been making besan Chillas forever and typically this gluten free high protein flour can be mixed with veggies and provides up to 10-12 grams of protein per pancake.

2

Choose Protein-Packed Snacks

  • Healthy Snacks: Reach for snacks like nuts, seeds, cheese, hard-boiled eggs, or a protein bar. These are convenient and high in protein, making them perfect for between meals.
  • Greek Yogurt: Choose plain Greek yogurt and add your own fruit or honey for flavor, which offers more protein than regular yogurt.
  • Cheese: Cheddar, cottage, mozzarella and even goat cheese may contain a lot of saturated fat, but they also contain a good amount of protein, 1.5 ounces can give you about 12 grams of protein.

3

Incorporate Protein in Every Meal

  • Lean Meats and Seafood: Include lean sources of protein like chicken, turkey, fish and shellfish in your meals. Grilled, baked, or broiled are healthier preparation methods.
  • Plant-Based Proteins: Add beans, lentils, tofu, or tempeh to your dishes. These are excellent plant-based protein sources. Yes they are not complete proteins with all amino acids and sometimes can be high in carbohydrates, but if you are a vegetarian it’s still important to get as much protein as you can to build your quality of life.
  • Eggs and Egg Whites: Use eggs in salads, wraps, and stir-fries. They can make a great snack but also turn it into an egg salad sandwich or an egg curry for a meal.

4

Enhance Your Meals with Protein-Rich Ingredients

  • Add Nuts and Seeds: Sprinkle chia seeds, flaxseeds, or hemp seeds on salads, smoothies, or yogurt for a protein boost.
  • Include Legumes: Incorporate beans, chickpeas, or peas into soups, stews, and salads for added protein.
  • Top with Cheese: Add a sprinkle of cheese to dishes like salads, soups, and casseroles for extra protein.

5

 Plan Ahead

  • Meal Prep: Prepare protein-rich meals in advance, so you always have a healthy option ready. This can include grilled chicken, tofu stir-fry, or a batch of quinoa salad.
  • Balanced Portions: Aim to include a source of protein with every meal. For instance, plan meals around a protein, whether it’s meat, fish, eggs, or legumes.

Almonds or nut butters

Legumes

Greek Yogurt

Seafood

item.title: Fiber Fix: Why Your Body Needs This Secret Superfood for a Healthy Gut
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Let’s not think of fiber as only used for old folks with digestive issues. Fiber has long proved to be essential for longevity. Fiber usually emerges as an unsung hero and is that one ingredient that is common in all blue zone regions.While the specific foods may differ, the common thread among Blue Zone diets is the emphasis on whole, plant-based foods that are naturally high in fiber. 

It's worth noting that fiber intake in Blue Zones often exceeds the recommended daily amounts in many Western countries. For example, the traditional Okinawan diet is estimated to contain 30-40 grams of fiber per day, significantly higher than the average American intake of about 15 grams, which is much lower than the 25 grams recommended by the FDA.

The Link Between Fiber and Longevity

1. Reduced Risk of Chronic Diseases:

High fiber intake is associated with a lower risk of heart disease, stroke, hypertension, certain gastrointestinal diseases, obesity, and type 2 diabetes. Studies have shown individuals consuming high amounts of fiber had a 15-30% reduction in mortality compared to those with lower fiber intake.

2. Improved Gut Health:

Fiber acts as a prebiotic, nourishing the beneficial bacteria in your gut, which is crucial for healthy digestion, a robust immune system and reducing inflammation. A healthy gut microbiome has shown to be essential for an overall health and longevity.

3. Blood Sugar Regulation:

Soluble fiber slows down the absorption of sugar, helping to prevent spikes in blood glucose and insulin levels. Fiber also improves the ability of your gut to absorb nutrients and vitamins more effectively by slowing down digestion.

4. Weight Management:

High-fiber foods tend to be more filling, and by keeping you full longer it helps control your appetite and reduce overall calorie intake which can be one important tool in your weight loss journey.

Tips for Increasing Fiber Intake Safely

Fiber may not be as sexy as protein, it’s not going to help you build those glamor muscles, but it will help make them visible and it is very essential to your everyday diet. It is a common component in just about any popular healthy lifestyle diet. It really will have one of the most immediate impacts of your life but you will have to be gradual with your methods as rushing too fast will cause discomfort and potentially embarrassment with gas and having to constantly run to the bathroom for those reasons it’s best to start slow.

  • Increase Gradually: To prevent digestive discomfort, increase your fiber intake slowly over several weeks.
  • Stay Hydrated: Drink plenty of water to help fiber move smoothly through your digestive system. This is usually the biggest mistake people make when trying to increase fiber and often leads to bloating.
  • Diversify Your Sources: Aim to get a mix of soluble and insoluble fiber from various foods for balanced benefits. A great way I will do this is by adding veggies to a pasta sauce or a chili. The way I try to increase fiber and protein is by combining it into a smoothie, add some chia or flax seeds and one meal can meet half your daily needs.
  • Read Food Labels: Look for products with at least 3 grams of fiber per serving. This means you may have to occasionally change from white to whole wheat bread, white to brown or cauliflower rice or just whole wheat pastas.
  • Take a Supplement: Sometimes you might be traveling or to meet your other goals, (ie: protein intake), may not be able to get enough fiber and there is nothing wrong with taking some psyllium husk, guar fiber or other fiber supplements to just give you that extra boost, just remember start slow.

Practical Ways to Add More Fiber to Your Diet

Here are three takeaways:

  • Start Your Day with Whole Grains
    • Opt for oatmeal or overnight oats with some protein powder, whole-wheat toast for breakfast with avocado. A veggie omelet can add fiber as well as protein first thing in the morning. 
    • Add berries or sliced bananas to your oatmeal for an extra fiber boost.
    • Greek Yogurt with some berries and chia seeds isa great way to add fiber, protein and good fats first thing in the morning.
  • Incorporate Legumes
    • Include beans, lentils, and chickpeas in salads, soups, and stews. You can even try chickpea pasta or pancakes, most likely your kids will notice but mix it with the sauce and it might be less noticeable.
    • For the next game add some variations of beans to the nachos place, black, refried or even garbanzo, smother it with some cheese and they won’t be able to tell the difference.
  • Snack on Fruits and Vegetables
    • Keep fresh fruits, raw veggies, or dried fruits handy for quick snacks, I have noticed if i leave fruit on the counter vs shoving in the fridge my kids are more likely to eat it.
    • Leave more of the skin on, leaving the skin on that kiwi will give your 50% more fiber, you don’t need to peel that apple, it will have more vitamin K and A with the peel on.
    • Pair apple slices with almond butter or enjoy carrot or cucumber sticks with hummus.
  • Add Seeds and Nuts
    • Sprinkle chia seeds, flaxseeds, or chopped nuts on yogurt, salads, or smoothies. Try almond flour pancakes or waffles once in a while, add some chocolate chips and the kids might not be able to tell.
  • Bake with Fiber in Mind
    • No one is telling you not to enjoy a treat here and there and who doesn’t like some delicious baked goods like oatmeal cookies. Using whole-grain flours or adding grated vegetables like zucchini or carrots to baked goods could be a great way to add some additional fiber. 
    • Try adding dates as a sweetener, in your baking as well in your smoothies. I never add sugar to my smoothies, but for the kids I have noticed adding a date or two just sweetens it up a bit so that they enjoy it that much more. A serving of dates can provide about 7-8 grams of fiber.

item.title: Unlock the Power of Magnesium: Why This Mighty Mineral Is Key to Better Health
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Magnesium is essential for your overall health, impacting everything from sleep quality to heart health and mental well-being. Whether through food or supplements, ensuring you get enough magnesium daily can make a significant difference in how you feel and function. For those who need an extra boost, choosing the right type of magnesium supplement based on your specific needs can help target areas like sleep, energy, or cognitive function.

Magnesium is a vital mineral that plays a critical role in numerous bodily functions. It’s involved in over 300 enzyme reactions, affecting everything from muscle function to mood regulation and heart health. Despite its importance, many people do not get enough magnesium in their diet. As we age our ability to absorb magnesium from food also decreases and certain medications, including diuretics, proton pump inhibitors (for acid reflux), and some antibiotics, can reduce magnesium levels. Which is why incorporating more magnesium-rich foods into your diet, such as leafy greens, nuts, seeds, and whole grains, is a natural and effective way to maintain optimal magnesium levels and support long-term health.

Let’s dig deeper into the many ways magnesium provides the support we need especially as we age and why it is so readily used from hospitals to functional medicine circles. 

The Benefits of Magnesium

Magnesium is that wonder mineral that you will almost certainly hear about regardless if its at a hospital, at your grandma's house or wellness shops. It is such a versatile mineral and so important in so many areas that makes it essential for healthy living.

The Benefits of Magnesium

  1. Supports Muscle and Nerve Function: Magnesium helps regulate muscle contractions and relaxes the muscles, which is important for preventing cramps, stiffness, and spasms. It's especially vital for athletes or individuals prone to muscle tightness, but also those that tend to be more prone to charlie horses or muscle cramps. Tip: Try taking a supplement post power workout.
  2. Boosts Bone Health: As we get older, bone density naturally decreases, especially in postmenopausal women and older men. Magnesium is vital for bone formation and helps regulate calcium and vitamin D, both of which are essential for bone health.
  3. Improves Sleep Quality: Magnesium helps activate neurotransmitters like GABA that calm the nervous system, and reduce the stress hormone cortisol which can improve sleep quality and duration.
  4. Enhances Mental Health: It’s linked to mood regulation and may help reduce anxiety and depression by balancing neurotransmitters in the brain. Low magnesium levels are associated with increased stress and mood disorders, which is more common as we age.
  5. Supports Energy Production: Magnesium helps convert food into energy, making it essential for reducing fatigue and maintaining overall vitality. A key relationship is the relationship it has with biochemical reactions in the body such as with protein synthesis and blood glucose control that support energy production.
  6. Supports PMS Symptoms: Magnesium has shown to help with relaxing the muscles and helping to reduce period cramps, headaches and bloating. A recent study showed magnesium may help prevent production of prostaglandins which reduce the severity of muscle cramps. Taking it along side B6 has shown even greater benefits but more research has to be done.

Types of Magnesium and Their Use Cases

How to Naturally Increase Magnesium Levels

1. Leafy Greens

Spinach, Swiss chard, and kale are excellent sources of magnesium. One cup of cooked spinach provides 157 mg of magnesium (about 40% of the recommended daily intake).

2. Nuts and Seeds

Almonds, cashews, and pumpkin seeds are magnesium-rich snacks. For instance, a quarter cup of almonds provides 96 mg of magnesium.

3. Whole Grains

Foods like brown rice, quinoa, and whole wheat products are good sources of magnesium. A cup of cooked quinoa offers around 118 mg of magnesium.

4. Legumes

Beans, lentils, and chickpeas are rich in magnesium. A cup of cooked black beans has around 120 mg of magnesium.

5. Fatty Fish

Fish like salmon, mackerel, and halibut are not only rich in omega-3 fatty acids but also offer a good dose of magnesium. A 100-gram serving of mackerel provides 97 mg of magnesium.

6. Fruits like Avocados and Bananas

Avocados are nutrient-dense, and one medium avocado offers about 58 mg of magnesium. Known for their potassium content, bananas also contain magnesium, with one medium banana providing about 32 mg.

7. Dark Chocolate

A 1-ounce piece of dark chocolate contains around 64 mg of magnesium. Opt for chocolate with at least 70% cocoa for the highest benefit.

item.title: Ashwagandha: An Ancient Herb for Modern Stress
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) is a medicinal herb traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, renowned for its adaptogenic properties, meaning it helps the body manage stress. It is also called "Indian ginseng" and has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for over 3,000 years. Ayurveda, which means "the science of life," is a traditional system of medicine that originated in India. In Ayurveda, ashwagandha is classified as a Rasayana, which refers to herbs and substances that promote longevity and rejuvenation. It is prized for its potential to help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. In modern clinical studies it has shown a positive effect on stress, anxiety and depression.

Top 7 Key Benefits of Ashwagandha:

Ashwagandha is a versatile herb with a long history of use in traditional medicine. Its potential benefits for stress reduction, cognitive function, and overall well-being make it a popular choice for those seeking natural remedies. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine it has been traditionally used to increase energy, improve concentration, and strengthen immunity. If you're considering incorporating ashwagandha into your routine, read further to see how it can help you.

1. Reduces Stress and Anxiety

Ashwagandha is most well known for its ability to reduce stress, that is the primary reason I first started taking it. Multiple studies have found that Ashwagandha helps reduce cortisol levels, a stress hormone that increases during chronic stress. One study published in the Indian Journal of Psychological Medicine demonstrated a significant reduction in stress and anxiety in subjects taking 300 mg of Ashwagandha twice daily over 60 days, compared to a placebo group.

When life brings high levels of stress due to multitasking, family, and work responsibilities. Ashwagandha’s adaptogenic properties help the body manage stress by reducing cortisol levels, a hormone linked to stress.

2. Improves Sleep Quality

Since Ashwagandha helps reduce stress a beneficial byproduct is improved sleep. With its calming and adaptogenic effects It works by improving overall sleep latency (the time it takes to fall asleep) and enhancing sleep quality.

Ashwagandha has a calming effect on the central nervous system, which may help those who experience restlessness, racing thoughts, or difficulty winding down at night. This effect makes it useful for people who have trouble calming their minds before sleep. It is best to try taking it in a tea or supplement 30 minutes before bed.

3. Enhances Cognitive Function

Ashwagandha has been shown to boost memory and cognitive performance. Studies have shown improved cognitive function, including memory and attention in participants who took ashwagandha. The herb contains compounds called withanolides, which are believed to reduce oxidative stress in the brain, a key factor in age-related cognitive decline. It has the ability to stimulate neurogenesis, the process of forming new neurons in the brain, a crucial capability we lose as we age.

4. Boosts Hormonal Health

For men ashwagandha has shown promising results of boosting testosterone and increasing sperm quality and count. For women it has shown the ability to balance estrogen levels and improve the regularity of menstrual cycles, and reduce symptoms of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). It mostly is able to accomplish these results by reducing cortisol levels and oxidative stress.

5. Supports Muscle Growth and Strength

Men and women who took Ashwagandha experienced significantly greater muscle mass and strength gains after resistance training compared to those who took a placebo. Ashwagandha promotes muscle growth by increasing testosterone levels, a critical hormone for muscle development in both men and women. It has the ability to boost protein synthesis, which is essential for repairing and building muscle tissues after a workout.

Studies have also shown Ashwagandha may increase your VO2Max, which is a measurement of the max amount of oxygen a person can use during intense activity. Higher Vo2Max has been directly correlated with a longer and better quality of life as we age.

6. Reduces Inflammation

Ashwagandha contains compounds like withanolides that have shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. Withanolides, in particular, have been studied for their ability to suppress pro-inflammatory cytokines. With its ability to reduce cortisol and directly helps by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

7. May Help Regulate Blood Sugar Levels

Ashwagandha has been found to improve insulin sensitivity and lower blood sugar levels by  improving the body's sensitivity to insulin, a hormone that helps regulate blood sugar levels by facilitating the uptake of glucose into cells.

Stress is often the culprit for many of our chronic health conditions and finding ways to reduce that stress either through exercise, meditation or the added aid of a herb like Ashwagandha can help you keep your insulin and blood sugar levels in check.

Dosage and Safety

  • Commonly recommended dosages range from 300 mg to 600 mg of ashwagandha extract, taken 1–2 times daily.
  • Ashwagandha is generally considered safe, though it's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional, especially for individuals who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have autoimmune conditions.

item.title: Besan Pancake (Indian-Style chickpea pancake)
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Besan Crepe (chickpea flour crepe)

Besan crepe is a savory, protein-packed, and fiber-rich pancake made from chickpea flour (besan) that is popular in Indian cuisine. It’s a breakfast item or a matter of fact any time of the day. I usually recommend it to my vegetarian friends who would like to increase their protein intake especially for breakfast without having to resort to eggs everyday or for those that are not eating meat at a particular time due to religious reasons. It's a great option for a quick, healthy meal and let’s explore some of those health benefits.

[gallery ids="433"]

Breakfast
Indian

item.title: Gut and Glow Power Smoothie
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Gut & Glow Power Smoothie

This combination is nutritious and great for your digestion, we are advised to add fiber with good probiotics but how about adding it with a heart healthy, protein packed smoothie for all day energy!

  • blender
  • 1/4 Cup sauerkraut
  • 4-5 Baby carrots
  • 1/3 Medium Apple
  • 1 Cup Strawberries
  • 2 tsp Fine Flaxseed Powder
  • 2 tbsp Almond Butter
  • 1 serving Protein Powder
  • 4-5 Florets Frozen Cauliflower
  • 1 1/2 cup Water (Add less or more based on how thick you want your smoothie to be)

Health Benefits:

  • Strawberries: Packed with vitamin C and antioxidants, good for immunity.
  • Sauerkraut: Probiotic-rich, aids digestion and boosts gut health.
  • Carrots: High in beta-carotene, supporting eye and skin health.
  • Apple: Provides fiber and antioxidants, promoting heart health.
  • Cauliflower: Low-calorie, fiber-rich, and supports digestion.
  • Almond Butter: Provides healthy fats and protein, good for satiety and heart health.
  • Protein Powder: Boosts muscle repair and overall recovery.
  • Flaxseed Powder: Rich in omega-3s, supporting heart health and reducing inflammation.

Let's dive a little more into each ingredient, highlighting the benefits and the studies that help support these benefits. 

1 Cup of Frozen Strawberries

   – Benefits: Strawberries are rich in antioxidants, particularly vitamin C and manganese. They help boost immune health, improve skin health, and reduce inflammation.

   – Studies: Consumption of strawberries is linked to improved heart health and reduced oxidative stress.

¼ cup Sauerkraut (Yea you read that right)

  • Benefits: Being fermented, sauerkraut is rich in probiotics, which are the beneficial bacteria that support gut health, digestion, and the immune system. A healthy gut can also positively impact mental health through the gut-brain connection.

These probiotics help maintain a balanced gut flora, aiding in digestion and reducing symptoms like bloating or constipation. Sauerkraut is high in vitamins C and K, which support immune function and bone health.

  • Studies: Regular consumption of sauerkraut has shown to improve immune function and protect against inflammation with the introduction of possible bacteria in the gut biome.

4-5 Baby Carrots

   – Benefits: Carrots are high in beta-carotene, which the body converts to vitamin A, crucial for good vision, immune function, and skin health. They also provide dietary fiber and several other vitamins and minerals.

   – Studies: Regular consumption of carrots is associated with improved eye health and reduced risk of certain cancers.

⅓ of an Apple

   – Benefits: Apples are rich in fiber, particularly pectin, which aids in digestion and helps maintain healthy blood sugar levels. They are also a good source of vitamin C and various antioxidants.

   – Studies: Apples have been linked to lower risk of chronic diseases, including cardiovascular disease.

 4-5 Florets of Cauliflower

   – Benefits: Cauliflower is such a great source of fiber and antioxidants. It is a great source of vitamin K that helps with calcium absorption for stronger bones. It's one of those superfoods that can fit into just about any diet plan, if you are keto, mediterranean or vegan.

– Studies: Cauliflower is included in the Cruciferous family and thus the components of cauliflower include glucosinolates, ascorbic acid, carotenoids, phenolic compounds and vitamin E. These vitamins and compounds give it its anticarcinogenic and antioxidant effects. Studies mostly have focused on the benefits of cauliflower and other cruciferous vegetables with lowering risk of cardiovascular, cancer and neurological disorders but new evidence is showing the benefits in bone health especially for perimenopausal women.

2 Tablespoon of Almond Butter

   – Benefits: Almond butter provides healthy fats, protein, fiber, and essential nutrients like vitamin E, magnesium, and potassium. It supports heart health, helps control blood sugar levels, and promotes satiety. I personally love the taste of almond butter, but you really can add any specific nut butter you prefer.

   – Studies: Nuts and nut butters have been linked to a lower risk of cardiovascular disease and improved blood lipid profiles.

1 Serving of Protein Powder

   – Benefits: Protein powder is an excellent source of high-quality protein, which is essential for muscle repair and growth. It helps keep you full and can aid in weight management.

   – Studies: Protein supplementation is beneficial for muscle recovery and overall muscle health, especially post-exercise.

1 Teaspoon of Fine Flaxseed Powder 

   – Benefits: Flaxseed oil is rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which support heart health, reduce inflammation, and promote brain function. It also provides fiber that aids in digestion.

   – Studies: Flaxseed oil consumption is associated with improved cardiovascular health and anti-inflammatory effects.

Smoothies
Healthy

item.title: Mango Carrot Dream Smoothie
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Mango Carrot Dream Smoothie

This smoothie is a powerhouse blend of fruits and veggies that offers a refreshing start to your day and best of all it tastes amazing! The mango and strawberries provide a burst of vitamin C and antioxidants, perfect for boosting skin health and immunity. Carrots and cauliflower add fiber and vitamin A for digestion and eye health and provide that creamy texture, while flaxseed powder and almond butter bring healthy fats and omega-3s and satiety. Collagen supports skin, hair, and joint health, making this smoothie a fantastic all-in-one wellness boost!

  • blender
  • 1/2 cup mangoes ((prefer frozen))
  • 5-6 Baby carrots
  • 1/2 cup Strawberries
  • 1 tbsp Fine Flaxseed Powder
  • 1 tsbp Almond Butter
  • 4-5 florets Frozen Cauliflower (Frozen will add texture and keep the strong flavors at bay.)
  • 1 serving Collagen
  • 1 cup Water ((Adjust based on thickness your desire))

Health Benefits:

  • Mangoes: High vitamin A and C, anti inflammatory, amazing for your digestion and gut microbes.
  • Carrots: High in beta-carotene, supports your eye and skin health.
  • Strawberries: Packed with vitamin C and antioxidants, good for immunity.
  • Flaxseed Powder: Rich in omega-3s, supporting heart health and reduces inflammation. (Paid Link)
  • Almond Butter: Provides healthy fats and protein, good for satiety and heart health.
  • Cauliflower: Low-calorie, fiber-rich, and supports digestion.
  • Collagen: provides essential amino acids that support skin elasticity and joint health. (Paid Link)

Ingredients and Health Benefits:

1/2 Cup of Frozen Strawberries

   – Benefits: Strawberries are rich in antioxidants, particularly vitamin C and manganese. They help boost immune health, improve skin health, and reduce inflammation.

   – Studies: Consumption of strawberries is linked to improved heart health and reduced oxidative stress.

½ Cup of Mangos (Frozen in the winter and fresh in the summer months)

   – Benefits: Mangos are high in vitamins A and C, which are known to reduce inflammation and help with immune function. They also provide dietary fiber and best of all they taste amazing, it really is one of the sweetest fruits, which is why you can’t overdo it.

   – Studies: Regular consumption of mangos is associated with improved gut health due to the fiber and polyphenols and reduced risk of certain cancers.

4-5 Baby Carrots

   – Benefits: Carrots are high in beta-carotene, which the body converts to vitamin A, crucial for good vision, immune function, and skin health. They also provide dietary fiber and several other vitamins and minerals.

   – Studies: Regular consumption of carrots is associated with improved eye health and reduced risk of certain cancers.

   – Benefits: Cauliflower is such a great source of fiber and antioxidants. It is a great source of vitamin K that helps with calcium absorption for stronger bones. It's one of those superfoods that can fit into just about any diet plan, if you are keto, mediterranean or vegan.

– Studies: Cauliflower is included in the Cruciferous family and thus the components of cauliflower include glucosinolates, ascorbic acid, carotenoids, phenolic compounds and vitamin E. These vitamins and compounds give it its anticarcinogenic and antioxidant effects. Studies mostly have focused on the benefits of cauliflower and other cruciferous vegetables with lowering risk of cardiovascular, cancer and neurological disorders but new evidence is showing the benefits in bone health especially for perimenopausal women.

1 Tablespoon of Almond Butter

   – Benefits: Almond butter provides healthy fats, protein, fiber, and essential nutrients like vitamin E, magnesium, and potassium. It supports heart health, helps control blood sugar levels, and promotes satiety.

   – Studies: Nuts and nut butters have been linked to a lower risk of cardiovascular disease and improved blood lipid profiles.

1 Serving of Collagen

   – Benefits: Collagen powder provides essential amino acids that support skin elasticity, joint health, and muscle repair. It helps improve skin hydration and reduce wrinkles.

    – Studies: Collagen supplementation has been shown to improve skin health, reduce joint pain and have been shown to help with bone and cartilage regeneration.

1 Teaspoon of Fine Flaxseed Powder 

   – Benefits: Flaxseed oil is rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which support heart health, reduce inflammation, and promote brain function. It also provides fiber that aids in digestion.

   – Studies: Flaxseed oil consumption is associated with improved cardiovascular health and anti-inflammatory effects.

Smoothies
Quick and Healthy

item.title: Berry Bean Protein Smoothie
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Berry Bean Protein Smoothie

This protein and fiber packed smoothie offers a balanced mix of protein, fiber, antioxidants, and healthy fats to keep you satisfied, energized, and feeling great! Perfect as a breakfast, post-workout snack, or midday boost. Don’t let the garbanzo (aka chickpeas) scare you, adding just a handful is enough to unlock their benefits without overpowering your smoothie.

  • blender
  • 1 cup  Mixed berries ((fresh or frozen))
  • 1/2 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1/3 Sliced and cored apple
  • 1/2 cup Spinach ((fresh or frozen))
  • 1/4 cup Garbanzo Beans ((soaked or canned will work))
  • 1 tbsp Fine Flaxseed Powder
  • 2 tbsp Almond Butter
  • 1 Protein Powder
  • 1 cup Water

Health Benefits:

  • Spinach: Packed with vitamins A, C, K, folate, iron, and calcium. It supports eye health, boosts immune function.
  • Garbanzo beans: High in fiber, protein and promotes heart health.
  • Greek Yogurt: Great source of protein and probiotics.
  • Apples: Provides fiber and antioxidants, promoting heart health.
  • Mixed Berries: Packed with vitamin C and antioxidants, good for immunity.
  • Flaxseed Powder: Rich in omega-3s, supporting heart health and reducing inflammation.
  • Almond Butter: Provides healthy fats and protein, good for satiety and heart health.
  • Protein Powder: Boosts muscle repair and overall recovery.

 

This combination is nutritious and great for your digestion, we are advised to add fiber with good probiotics but how about adding it with a heart healthy, protein packed smoothie for all day energy!

 

Ingredients and Health Benefits:

1 Cup of mixed berries

 - Benefits: Mixed berries, such as blueberries, strawberries, and raspberries, are rich in antioxidants, particularly vitamin C and anthocyanins. These antioxidants help combat oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and support overall immune health.

   - Studies: Research shows that regular consumption of berries is associated with improved heart health and cognitive function.

2/3 Cup Frozen Spinach

   - Benefits: Spinach is packed with vitamins A, C, and K, as well as folate, iron, and calcium. It supports eye health, boosts immune function, and helps maintain healthy blood pressure levels.

   - Studies: Spinach's high antioxidant content provides cardiovascular benefits and reduces oxidative stress  .

⅓ of an Apple

   – Benefits: Apples are rich in fiber, particularly pectin, which aids in digestion and helps maintain healthy blood sugar levels. They are also a good source of vitamin C and various antioxidants.

   – Studies: Apples have been linked to lower risk of chronic diseases, including cardiovascular disease.

½ cup of Greek Yogurt

   - Benefits: Greek yogurt is a great source of protein, probiotics, calcium, and vitamin B12. It supports gut health by promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and aids in digestion.

   - Studies: Probiotics in Greek yogurt have been shown to improve digestive health and enhance immune function  .

2 Tablespoons of Almond Butter

   – Benefits: Almond butter provides healthy fats, protein, fiber, and essential nutrients like vitamin E, magnesium, and potassium. It supports heart health, helps control blood sugar levels, and promotes satiety.

   – Studies: Nuts and nut butters have been linked to a lower risk of cardiovascular disease and improved blood lipid profiles.

¼ cup Garbanzo beans

–Benefits: Garbanzo beans are sometimes known as the super bean because of all the benefits they provide. They are a great source of fiber, protein, antioxidants and a good source of iron. Not only has it been shown to be good for your gut and heart but also is a top contender of food eaten within the Blue Zones. 

– Studies: Garbanzo beans have been shown to lower diabetes and help women specifically with low iron levels especially for those that tend to be vegan or vegetation.

1 Serving of Protein Powder

   – Benefits: Protein powder is an excellent source of high-quality protein, which is essential for muscle repair and growth. It helps keep you full and can aid in weight management.

   – Studies: Protein supplementation is beneficial for muscle recovery and overall muscle health, especially post-exercise.

1 Teaspoon of Fine Flaxseed Powder 

   – Benefits: Flaxseed oil is rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which support heart health, reduce inflammation, and promote brain function. It also provides fiber that aids in digestion.

   – Studies: Flaxseed oil consumption is associated with improved cardiovascular health and anti-inflammatory effects.

Smoothies
Healthy and Delicious

item.title: Vitamin D: The Super Nutrient You Get for Free
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Vitamin D: Are you getting enough?

Vitamin D is a key nutrient that supports bone strength, immune health, and cognitive function. As we age, maintaining adequate vitamin D levels is vital for preventing serious health issues. One of the most affordable ways to boost vitamin D is through sun exposure—a simple walk during daylight can make a big difference. But if you're indoors most of the day, supplementation is a convenient and inexpensive option. Whether from sunlight, food, or supplements, making vitamin D a priority can greatly benefit your overall well-being.

How Vitamin D Helps

  1. Bone Health: Vitamin D helps the body absorb calcium, which is vital for building and maintaining strong bones. It reduces the risk of osteoporosis, fractures, and bone deformities like rickets in children and osteomalacia in adults.
  2. Immune Function: It supports the immune system, helping the body fend off infections and reduce inflammation. Research shows that vitamin D can play a role in protecting against illnesses like the flu, colds, and even autoimmune diseases. A recent study showed those with adequate levels of Vitamin D had decreased risks of contracting Covid-19.
  3. Mood and Mental Health: Vitamin D is linked to the regulation of mood and helps prevent conditions like depression and anxiety. Low levels are often associated with Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD) and other mood disorders.
  4. Heart Health: Some studies suggest that adequate vitamin D levels can help regulate blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease by improving vascular health.
  5. Muscle Function: It contributes to muscle strength and function, reducing the risk of falls, particularly in older adults. Low levels of vitamin D can lead to muscle weakness, pain, and even atrophy over time.

Best Ways to Get Vitamin D

  1. Sunlight Exposure: The skin synthesizes vitamin D when exposed to UVB rays from the sun. About 10-30 minutes of midday sun a few times a week can be sufficient for many people, depending on skin type, location, and time of year. Ideally if you want to limit your exposure to midday sun, trying to get more sun in the morning or dawn, also known as sunlight optimization could be beneficial with minimal risks of extreme sun exposure to avoid skin cancer risks. Morning sunlight also has the added benefit of regulating your circadian rhythm.
  2. Food Sources: Certain foods naturally contain or are fortified with vitamin D:
    • Fatty fish like salmon, mackerel, and sardines
    • Egg yolks
    • Fortified milk and dairy products
    • Mushrooms
    • Cod liver oil
  3. Supplements: For individuals who don't get enough from sunlight or food, vitamin D supplements can help maintain optimal levels. The recommended daily intake can vary, but for most adults, 600-800 IU per day is typical, with higher doses sometimes recommended for older adults or those with severe deficiencies, especially based on your geo location. If you tend to live in the northern states that don’t get too much sun in the winter months you might want to increase your dosage to 2000-5000 IU.

When choosing vitamin D supplements, you'll find two forms: vitamin D2 and vitamin D3. Vitamin D2, derived from plants, is present in fortified foods and some supplements, while vitamin D3, naturally produced by the body, comes from animal-based foods. While both forms can increase vitamin D levels, studies suggest that D3 (cholecalciferol) is more effective at raising and maintaining blood levels compared to D2 (ergocalciferol). For this reason, many experts recommend D3 as the preferred option, as it aligns with the body’s natural processes and is commonly found in foods that naturally contain the vitamin.

Effects of Vitamin D Deficiency with Age

As people age, the risk of vitamin D deficiency increases due to several factors, including reduced skin synthesis, less time spent outdoors, and decreased absorption. Here are some of the key effects of not getting enough vitamin D as you age:

Bone Weakness and Fractures: Vitamin D deficiency can lead to brittle bones, increasing the risk of osteoporosis and fractures, particularly in postmenopausal women and older men. This can result in significant mobility issues and a higher risk of falls. Without enough vitamin D, the body struggles to absorb and use calcium effectively, leading to low bone mineral density.

Muscle Weakness: Low vitamin D levels contribute to muscle weakness and discomfort, increasing fall and injury risks. Coupled with age-related muscle loss (sarcopenia), which results in approximately 5% muscle loss per decade after 30 and accelerates to around 8% per decade after 60, the impact is significant. Prioritizing vitamin D intake along with regular strength training can help maintain muscle health and prevent age-related declines in strength and mobility.

Immune System Decline: A deficiency can impair immune function, making older adults more susceptible to infections like the flu, pneumonia or even covid.

Cognitive Decline: Emerging research links low vitamin D levels to cognitive decline and an increased risk of dementia and Alzheimer’s disease. Vitamin D supports brain function by reducing inflammation, promoting nerve cell health, and aiding in neuroprotection. Studies suggest that individuals with adequate vitamin D levels have better memory, focus, and overall cognitive performance. 

Increased Risk of Chronic Diseases: Vitamin D deficiency has been associated with higher risks of chronic conditions like cardiovascular disease, hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and certain cancers (like colorectal cancer).

item.title: Eggs with Prosciutto and Avocado on a Almond Tortilla
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Eggs with Prosciutto and Avocado on Almond Tortilla

Eggs with Prosciutto and Avocado on Almond Tortilla is a quick and delicious recipe that combines rich flavors and wholesome ingredients for a satisfying meal. The creamy avocado pairs perfectly with savory prosciutto and protein-packed eggs, all served on a soft, gluten-free almond tortilla.

  • Frying Pan
  • 2 Almond Tortillas
  • 2 Eggs
  • 2 Slices Prosciutto
  • 1 Avocado
  • 1/4 cup Cheddar Cheese ((Optional) Remove if vegan)
  • 1-2 tbsp Olive or avocado oil
  • pinch Salt (To Taste)
  • pinch Pepper (To Taste)
  • Red pepper flakes or hot sauce ((Optional))

  1. Prepare the Almond Tortillas: Warm the almond tortillas in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 30 seconds on each side, or until pliable and lightly toasted. Set aside.

  2. Cook the Eggs: Heat olive oil or avocado oil in a skillet over medium heat. Crack the eggs into the pan and cook them to your preference—sunny side up, over easy, or scrambled. Lightly season with salt and pepper.

  3. Prepare the Toppings: Slice the avocado and set it aside, next tear the prosciutto slices into smaller pieces for easier assembly.

  4. Assemble the Tortillas: Place each warmed almond tortilla on a plate. Add a layer of baby arugula or spinach on top of the tortilla.

    Lay the avocado slices evenly over the greens.

  5. Add Protein: Gently place a cooked egg on top of each tortilla, note: you can always mix in veggies to the scrambled eggs to add some additional fiber. Some great quick mix-ins include spinach, mushrooms, peppers, and onions, but add what you enjoy or happen to have in the fridge.

    Arrange the torn prosciutto slices around the egg. You can really use any protein option you like here, sausages, bacon, veggie patties or smoked salmon, the goal is to add some protein to keep you full longer.

  6. Finish with Toppings: Sprinkle mozzarella or cheddar cheese (optional) to add another layer of depth and flavor. I personally love a few sprinklers of cheddar cheese but totally your preference.

  7. Serve and Enjoy: Serve immediately as a quick, flavorful breakfast or light lunch option.

This is one of my favorites for a few reasons, first most it tastes amazing, and secondly it is so simple and quick to put together. It is a breakfast meal my kids love and it’s usually quicker than whipping together pancakes and so much healthier. This option is gluten free and you can easily be dairy free. You are combining some protein and choline from the eggs and the prosciutto, as well as a good source of vitamins b2, b3, b6, b12 and zinc. One of the other reasons I love this is because prosciutto is so flavorful that you don’t need a lot to add a pop to your meal. One slice typically has about 4 grams of protein and about 1.75 grams of unsaturated fat. 

Concerns: Yes it is true that the World health Organization (WHO) has classified all processed meat as a carcinogen and they have been linked to cancer in some studies. For that reason we don’t advocate making prosciutto or any processed meat as part of your everyday diet, but occasionally it can be enjoyed as a treat. For anyone that visits Italy or Spain you will notice Prosciutto and ham are regularly consumed, yet the colon cancer rates are significantly lower than other countries. Prosciutto is still considered one of the healthier of the processed meats, as it tends to have less saturated fat than other processed meats. Take prosciutto's 1.5 grams of saturated fat per ounce compared to pan-fried bacon (3.4 grams) or pork sausage (2.2 grams). If you decide this is not a risk you want to take you can always just remove it altogether or find a low fat substitute such as turkey.

 

Recipe Notes

  • Tortilla Options: I am a huge fan of the Siete Almond tortillas, which are regularly available in just about any grocery store these days, but you can also use cassava or regular tortilla.
  • Egg Cooking Method: Adjust the cooking method of the eggs based on your preference. Scrambled eggs are my go to mainly because it's so quick, while fried eggs have a crispy edge.
  • Avocado Ripeness: Choose a ripe avocado for easier mashing. It should yield slightly when pressed. If you don’t have time to mash the avocado, scooping out half and slicing it is a great alternative, just sprinkle a little salt and pepper to taste.
  • Seasoning Variations: Feel free to adapt the seasoning based on your taste. Add a pinch of chili flakes for heat or fresh herbs like spinach for extra flavor.

Meal Prep: The avocado can brown quickly; if preparing in advance, consider adding lemon juice to the mashed avocado to help preserve its color.

Breakfast
Dairy Free, Gluten Free, Healthy and Delicious

item.title: Savory Smoked Salmon Avocado Toast
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Savory Smoked Salmon Avocado Toast

Elevate your breakfast or brunch game with this quick and simple Savory Smoked Salmon Avocado Toast! Creamy avocado meets the smoky richness of salmon, layered on a crispy slice of artisan bread. Filled with good fats, and protein with or without eggs.

  • 2 Slices of sourdough or whole grain bread
  • 1 Avocado
  • 4 oz Smoked Salmon
  • 1 Small Tomato (sliced)
  • 1/4 Small Red Onion (thinly sliced)
  • 1 cup Argula
  • 1-2 tsp Everything Bagel Seasoning
  • 1 tbsp Olive Oil
  • Pinch Salt (to taste)
  • Pinch Pepper (to taste)

  1. Toast the Bread: Preheat your oven or toaster oven to 400°F (200°C). Place the slices of baguette or whole grain bread on a baking sheet and toast until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Alternatively, use a toaster.

  2. Prepare the Avocado: While the bread is toasting, cut the avocado in half, remove the pit, and scoop the flesh into a bowl. Mash it with a fork until creamy. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

  3. Season and Serve: Sprinkle everything bagel seasoning over the top and drizzle with a little olive oil if desired. Enjoy!

  4. Add Some Eggs: (Optional) Eggs are a great source of protein, but i left it optional as sometimes folks just want a breakfast without eggs. In a skillet, cook the eggs according to your preference. For poached eggs, bring a pot of water to a simmer, add a splash of vinegar, and gently crack the eggs into the water. Cook for about 3-4 minutes until the whites are set.

  5. Assemble the Toast: Spread a generous layer of the mashed or chopped avocado on each slice of toasted bread.

    Top with smoked salmon, followed by slices of tomato and red onions.

  6. Tips: For added flavor, you can drizzle a bit of lemon juice over the avocado before assembling the toast.

    Feel free to play with other toppings like cucumber or radishes for an extra crunch!

Elevate your breakfast, brunch, or light lunch with this Savory Smoked Salmon Avocado Toast—a dish that perfectly balances flavor and nutrition. Rich in healthy fats from creamy avocado, omega-3 fatty acids from delicate smoked salmon, and packed with vitamins from fresh tomatoes and peppery arugula, this recipe is as nourishing as it is delicious. A sprinkle of everything bagel seasoning adds a delightful crunch and deepens the savory flavor profile, making this toast a satisfying, wholesome choice to fuel your day.

 

Recipe Notes

  • Bread Options: You can use any type of bread you prefer, such as sourdough or rye, for different flavors and textures.
  • Avocado Ripeness: Choose a ripe avocado for easier mashing. It should yield slightly when pressed. If you don’t have time to mash the avocado, scooping out half and slicing it is a great alternative, just sprinkle a little salt and pepper to taste.
  • Seasoning Variations: Feel free to adapt the seasoning based on your taste. Add a pinch of chili flakes for heat or fresh herbs like dill for extra flavor.
  • Toppings: You can add other toppings like cucumber, radishes, or sprouts for additional crunch and nutrition.
  • Meal Prep: The avocado can brown quickly; if preparing in advance, consider adding lemon juice to the mashed avocado to help preserve its color.
Breakfast
American
Quick and Healthy

item.title: Healthy Quick Chicken Caesar Wrap
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Lean & Green Chicken Caesar Wrap

A high protein delicious wrap that comes together in minutes and tastes amazing

  • Pan
  • 1 (4-6 oz) boneless, skinless chicken breast (A quick alternative is to purchase fajita chicken in your deli section)
  • 1-2 tbsp Olive Oil (For grilling the Chicken)
  • Salt and Pepper to taste for the chicken
  • 1 Low Carb Tortilla (I have been loving the stone milled tortillas with 4 grams of plant protein.)
  • 1/2 cup Cup chopped Romaine Lettuce (Substitute with spinach or whatever greens you love)
  • 1/4 Sliced Tomato
  • 1/4 Ripe Avocado (Sliced or Smashed)
  • 1-2 tbsp Tablespoons of your Favorite Mayo
  • Sprinkle some Parmesan Cheese

  1. Grill Your Chicken: Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper. Grill over medium heat until fully cooked (internal temperature of 165°F/74°C). Let rest, then slice into strips. Pro Tip: Use leftover cooked chicken for an even faster lunch!

    For those days I really don’t have time, you can buy precooked fajita or grilled chicken, usually found near the salad and dressing aisle.

  2. Prep the Freshness: Wash and chop the romaine lettuce. Slice the tomato and avocado.

  3. Lay the Foundation: Place your low-carb wrap flat on a clean surface.

  4. Add the Creamy Base: Spread the mayonnaise (or avocado mayo) evenly in the center of the wrap.

  5. Layer in the Veggies: Top the mayo with the chopped romaine lettuce and sliced tomato.

  6. Pile on the Protein: Arrange the sliced grilled chicken over the vegetables.

  7. (Optional) Cheese Touch: Sprinkle a small amount of Parmesan cheese for added flavor.

  8. Wrap it Right: Fold in the sides of the low-carb tortilla, then roll it up tightly from the bottom.

  9. Serve and Savor: Slice your Lean & Green Chicken Caesar Wrap in half and enjoy immediately!

Fuel Your Day the Healthy Way: Lean & Green Chicken Caesar Wrap Recipe

Looking for a healthy and delicious lunch recipe that won't derail your wellness goals? We have you covered! Our Lean & Green Chicken Caesar Wrap delivers all the satisfying flavors of a classic Caesar salad in a convenient, low-carb package. Whether you are running back from a lunch gym session or just have non stop meetings and need a quick meal we got your covered. Packed with lean protein, vibrant veggies, and healthy fats, this wrap is your new go-to for a guilt-free lunch. 

Why This Lean Chicken Caesar Wrap is Your New Lunch Hero:

  • High in Lean Protein: Grilled chicken breast fuels your muscles and keeps you feeling full longer.
  • Low-Carb Friendly: Use your favorite low-carb wrap or a Siete Almond tortilla to help you manage your carbohydrate intake.
  • Packed with Freshness: Crisp greens such as lettuce or spinach and juicy tomatoes provide essential vitamins and fiber.
  • Healthy Fats for Satiety: Creamy avocado adds heart-healthy monounsaturated fats and helps you stay full longer.

 

Quick and Easy: Perfect for busy weekdays, this wrap comes together in minutes. You can even make it the night before and have it ready to go.

 

Self Health’s Smart Swaps & Flavor Boosts:

  • Customize Your Veggies: Feel free to add other low-carb vegetables like cucumber or bell peppers.
  • Vegetarian Option: Swap the chicken for grilled halloumi or seasoned tofu for a meat-free alternative.

This Lean & Green Chicken Caesar Wrap is more than just a lunch; it's a step towards a healthier you! It's quick, easy, and packed with the good stuff your body craves. So, ditch the carb-heavy options and embrace this flavorful, low-carb lunch today!

Share your delicious creations with us using #SelfHealthLivingLunch – we love seeing your healthy eating wins!

 

Lunch
Quick and Healthy

item.title: Fiery Chicken and Black Bean Tostadas
item.dc:creator: mkhnqpmy
item.content:encoded:
Are you craving a meal that's both delicious and good for you? Look no further! Today, we're bringing you a recipe for Chicken and Black Bean Tostadas – a fiesta of flavor and nutrients that's easy to make and perfect for a light lunch or dinner. Get ready for crispy tostadas piled high with savory chicken, hearty black beans, and fresh, vibrant toppings!

Fiery Chicken and Black Bean Tostadas

A burst of flavors, high in protein and quick meal

  • Pan or skillet
  • Oven
  • Pot
  • 1 6-8 Oz Chicken Breast
  • 1 tbsp Avocado or Olive Oil
  • 1/2 tsp chilli powder
  • 2 tsp Taco Seasoning (I love the Siete Spicy)
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 tsp Salt and peper (Adjust to your taste)
  • 4 Corn Tortillas
  • 1 15 Oz Can of Black Beans (Rinsed and Drained)
  • 1/4 cup Salsa
  • 1/2 cup Shredded Lettuce
  • 1/4 cup Red Onions
  • 1/2 Ripe Avocado
  • 1/4 cup Diced Tomatoes
  • Fresh cilantro, a dollop of sour cream, a squeeze of lime juice or some fresh jalapeños for a little kick.

  1. Cook the Chicken: Heat the avocado oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the chicken breast and season with chili powder, taco seasoning (it’s just so convenient), salt, and black pepper. Cook for about 6-8 minutes per side, or until cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Once cooked, you can shred the chicken or just chop it up like at Chipotle. Shortcut: Use leftover cooked or rotisserie chicken!

  2. Warm the Black Beans: In the same skillet (or a separate one), gently heat the rinsed and drained black beans over low heat. You can mash some of the beans with a fork for a creamier texture if desired. Stir in the salsa and heat through.

  3. Prepare the Tostadas: You have a few options here:

    Bake: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Lightly brush the corn tortillas with a little avocado oil (optional) and bake for 8-10 minutes, or until crispy.

    Broil: Place the corn tortillas on a baking sheet and broil for 1-2 minutes per side, watching carefully to prevent burning.

    Pan-fry: Heat a thin layer of oil in a skillet over medium heat. Fry the corn tortillas for 1-2 minutes per side until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.

  4. Assemble Your Tostadas: Once the tostadas are crispy, it's time to build!

    Spoon a generous amount of the warm black bean mixture onto each tostada

    Top with the chicken

    Add a layer of shredded lettuce and chopped red onion and tomatoes

    Finish with diced or sliced avocado (for the added flavor I typically just make some guac and add on top).

  5. Add Your Favorite Flair: Sprinkle with fresh cilantro or jalapeños, add a dollop of sour cream and a squeeze of fresh lime juice for a zesty finish.

Why We Love These Healthy Chicken and Black Bean Tostadas:

  • Lean Protein Power: Chicken provides essential protein for muscle health and satiety.
  • Fiber-Rich Black Beans: Black beans are packed with fiber, promoting healthy digestion and keeping you feeling full.
  • Customizable & Fun: Tostadas are a blank canvas! You can easily adjust the toppings to your liking. Picky kids, no problem! My kids love this dish, if i can’t get them to load up on the veggies at least i know they will get some chicken and guacamole.
  • Quick & Easy: This recipe comes together in a snap, especially if you have pre-cooked chicken.
  • Naturally Gluten-Free (if using corn tortillas): Perfect for those with gluten sensitivities.

 

Self Health Living's Smart Swaps & Flavor Boosts:

  • Spice it Up: Add a pinch of cayenne pepper to your chicken seasoning or use a spicier salsa.
  • Veggie Boost: Add other healthy toppings like corn kernels, bell peppers and onions (diced and sautéed) or whatever you are feeling today.
  • Leaner Protein Options: You can also make this with shredded turkey or plant-based protein crumbles. With ground lean turkey, I will sometimes add some cheese into it just to make it a little less dry and flavorful.
  • Control the Sodium: Rinse your canned black beans thoroughly to reduce sodium content.

These Healthy Chicken and Black Bean Tostadas are a delicious and nutritious way to enjoy a satisfying meal. They're packed with protein, fiber, and healthy fats, keeping you feeling energized and happy. So, gather your ingredients, get creative with your toppings, and enjoy this flavorful fiesta on a crispy tortilla!

Dinner, Lunch
Mexican
Healthy and Delicious

item.title: Your Glutes: The Unsung Powerhouse for Lifelong Health & Vitality
item.dc:creator: health_agent
item.content:encoded:
When you think about muscles crucial for a long, healthy life, what comes to mind? Perhaps your heart, your core, or even your biceps for everyday tasks. But a recent article in The New York Times highlighted an 'unsung hero' muscle, reminding us that sometimes the most vital components of our longevity are the ones we least expect. Today, we're diving into why your glutes – yes, your glutes – are this very powerhouse, and how strengthening them can profoundly impact your healthspan and vitality, especially as you navigate life beyond 40.

The Longevity Lesson: Your Glutes Are Your Foundation

It's easy to dismiss your glutes (the muscles of your buttocks) as primarily aesthetic, but their role in your overall health and longevity is truly foundational. Think of them as the unsung architects of your body's stability, power, and movement efficiency. Comprising three major muscles – the gluteus maximus, medius, and minimus – they work together to stabilize your pelvis, power your walking, running, and climbing, and support your spine.

Empirical evidence consistently shows that strong glutes are correlated with better balance, reduced risk of lower back pain, improved posture, and even enhanced metabolic health. They are key players in maintaining functional independence, ensuring you can continue to enjoy activities like hiking, dancing, or simply playing with grandchildren without pain or limitation for decades to come.

Why Strong Glutes Matter Immensely for You, Over 40

As we age, a natural process called sarcopenia, or age-related muscle loss, begins. This isn't just about losing strength; it impacts your mobility, increases your risk of falls, and can even slow your metabolism. Your glutes, being one of the largest and most powerful muscle groups, are uniquely positioned to combat these age-related declines. Neglecting them can lead to:

  • Poor Posture: Weak glutes often cause other muscles (like your hip flexors) to compensate, pulling your pelvis forward and leading to a slouched posture.
  • Back and Knee Pain: When your glutes aren't doing their job, the strain often shifts to your lower back and knees, leading to chronic discomfort and injury.
  • Reduced Mobility and Balance: Weak glutes compromise your ability to push off, stabilize yourself, and prevent falls, which becomes increasingly critical with age.
  • Lower Metabolic Rate: More muscle mass, especially in large groups like the glutes, means a higher resting metabolism, helping manage weight and overall energy.

By actively strengthening your glutes, you're not just building muscle; you're investing in your future self – ensuring sustained energy, robust mobility, and a higher quality of life for years to come.

Actionable Steps for Stronger Glutes & a Longer Healthspan

The good news? You don't need fancy equipment or a gym membership to start building stronger glutes. Consistency and proper form are your best allies. Here are 3 practical steps you can integrate into your routine:

  • Master the Glute Bridge: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor hip-width apart, and arms by your sides. Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to lift your hips off the floor until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. Hold for a second, then slowly lower. Start with 3 sets of 10-15 repetitions, focusing on the glute squeeze at the top.
  • Perfect Your Bodyweight Squat: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly out. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back as if sitting in a chair, keeping your chest up and core engaged. Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor (or as deep as comfortable with good form), then push through your heels to return to standing, squeezing your glutes at the top. Aim for 3 sets of 10-12 repetitions.
  • Walk with Intention: Regular walking is fantastic, but you can make it a glute-building exercise. Focus on pushing off through your heels and actively squeezing your glutes with each stride. Seek out hills or varied terrains to increase the challenge. Consider adding a few short walking intervals where you exaggerate this glute engagement.

Incorporating these simple, effective exercises into your weekly routine can make a profound difference in how you feel, move, and thrive. Your glutes are indeed an unsung hero of longevity – give them the attention they deserve, and they'll reward you with a stronger, more vibrant healthspan.

Source: Read the original article
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover why one often-overlooked muscle group—your glutes—is critical for longevity, vitality, and healthspan after 40. Learn actionable steps to strengthen them.

item.title: Unlock Your Health Data: How AI is Reshaping Longevity
item.dc:creator: health_agent
item.content:encoded:
The world of health and longevity is evolving at warp speed, and nowhere is this more apparent than in the intersection of personal data and artificial intelligence. A recent fascinating piece in the Wall Street Journal highlighted a growing trend: individuals uploading their personal blood work to AI platforms for deeper analysis, asking the intriguing question, "Am I Oversharing?" For those of us aged 40 and beyond, dedicated to optimizing our healthspan – the number of years we live in good health – this isn't just a tech trend; it's a potential game-changer. It signals a shift from reactive medicine to proactive, data-driven self-care. While the idea of sharing intimate health data might raise eyebrows, the potential for personalized insights is truly compelling.

AI: Your New Health Detective?

Traditionally, a doctor reviews your blood work, flags anything outside the "normal" range, and offers advice. This is crucial, but it often provides a snapshot rather than a predictive trajectory. What AI brings to the table, as explored in the WSJ article, is the ability to sift through vast amounts of data – not just your current numbers, but historical trends, genetic predispositions (if provided), and even population-level health data – to identify subtle patterns and potential risks long before they become critical. Think of it as having a super-powered health detective analyzing your personal biomarkers. It’s not about replacing your doctor, but empowering you with a more nuanced understanding of your body. This deeper analysis can pinpoint nutritional deficiencies, metabolic imbalances, or early indicators of chronic disease risks that might otherwise go unnoticed until symptoms emerge. For instance, an AI might highlight a creeping rise in a certain inflammatory marker over several years, prompting early lifestyle adjustments that could prevent future health issues. This is the core "Longevity Lesson" here: Leveraging advanced technology like AI can transform your health journey from a reactive response to proactive, personalized optimization. It allows you to move beyond "normal" ranges to strive for optimal ranges tailored to your unique physiology, helping you make informed decisions to extend both your lifespan and, more importantly, your healthspan.

How to Improve Your Healthspan Today

While cutting-edge AI tools are becoming more accessible, there are concrete steps you can take right now to embrace a data-driven approach to your health:
  • Get Regular Comprehensive Blood Work: Don't wait for a problem. Work with your doctor to get annual or semi-annual comprehensive blood panels. Discuss key markers beyond the basics, such as C-reactive protein (inflammation), HbA1c (blood sugar control), Vitamin D levels, and a full lipid panel. Understanding these numbers is your first step to informed action.
  • Explore Reputable Health Data Platforms (with Caution): If you're comfortable and mindful of privacy, research emerging platforms that offer AI-powered insights for health data. Look for services that prioritize data security, provide clear disclaimers that they are not medical advice, and encourage consultation with your physician. Remember, these are tools for insight, not diagnoses.
  • Master the Foundations: No AI tool can substitute for the basics. Consistently prioritize nutrient-dense whole foods, aim for 7-9 hours of quality sleep nightly, engage in regular physical activity (a mix of cardio, strength, and flexibility), and manage stress effectively. These fundamental habits are the bedrock upon which all other health optimizations are built.
Embracing technology in health isn't about giving up control; it's about gaining more insight to make smarter choices. With AI as a partner, we can look forward to a future where living a long, vibrant, and healthy life isn't just a dream, but a well-informed reality. Source: Read the original article
item.excerpt:encoded: Curious about AI for health? Discover how uploading blood work, a trend highlighted by WSJ, offers personalized insights for a longer, vibrant life.

item.title: Your Glucose, Your Longevity: Leveraging Tech for a Healthier You
item.dc:creator: health_agent
item.content:encoded:
In our journey to live longer, healthier, and more vibrant lives, understanding our body's inner workings is paramount. That's why recent news about Medicare's expanding coverage of Continuous Glucose Monitors (CGMs) for eligible individuals with diabetes is a significant development. While this directly impacts those managing diabetes, it signals a broader recognition: glucose management isn't just about managing a disease; it's a cornerstone of overall healthspan and vitality for everyone.

Consider this: over 1 in 3 American adults currently have prediabetes, and most don't even know it. This staggering statistic underscores why proactive metabolic health, like the kind CGMs can illuminate, is so crucial for adults aged 40 and beyond.

Beyond Diabetes: The Power of Personalized Glucose Data

For too long, glucose monitoring was primarily associated with a diabetes diagnosis. However, pioneering health and longevity experts now emphasize that stable blood sugar is fundamental to energy levels, mood, cognitive function, inflammation control, and graceful aging. Fluctuations in glucose, even outside diabetic ranges, can contribute to fatigue, brain fog, weight gain, and increased risk for chronic diseases down the line.

This is where new health tech like CGMs becomes a powerful optimization tool. Instead of just snapshot blood tests, CGMs provide real-time, continuous insights into how your body responds to food, exercise, stress, and sleep. This personalized data empowers you to make informed, immediate adjustments to your lifestyle, moving from guesswork to precision health. You can see exactly which foods spike your blood sugar, how a morning walk impacts your levels, or how a stressful meeting affects your metabolic response. This isn't about being perfect; it's about being informed and empowered to make choices that support your longevity.

How to Improve Your Healthspan Today

You don't need a medical diagnosis to take control of your metabolic health. Here are actionable steps you can start right now:

  • Explore Glucose Monitoring Options: Talk to your doctor about your metabolic health. While Medicare coverage for CGMs is currently for those with diabetes requiring intensive insulin therapy (as highlighted by AARP), there are personal-use CGMs available that can give you invaluable insights into your unique metabolic responses. Even periodic lab tests (fasting glucose, HbA1c, insulin) can provide a baseline.
  • Prioritize Nutrient-Dense Whole Foods: Focus on a diet rich in fiber from vegetables, fruits, and whole grains, combined with lean proteins and healthy fats. This combination helps stabilize blood sugar, prevents spikes and crashes, and nourishes your body at a cellular level. Reduce processed foods, added sugars, and refined carbohydrates, which are notorious for disrupting glucose balance.
  • Embrace Consistent Movement: Regular physical activity is a potent medicine for metabolic health. Even short walks after meals can significantly improve insulin sensitivity and help your body manage glucose more effectively. Aim for a mix of aerobic exercise and strength training throughout your week.
  • Optimize Sleep and Stress Management: Often overlooked, poor sleep and chronic stress profoundly impact glucose regulation. Prioritize 7-9 hours of quality sleep and integrate stress-reducing practices like mindfulness, deep breathing, or spending time in nature. Your metabolic health will thank you.

By understanding and actively managing your glucose levels, you're not just preventing disease; you're building a foundation for sustained energy, mental clarity, and a vibrant healthspan. The future of longevity is proactive, personalized, and powered by informed choices.

Source: Read the original article
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover how Continuous Glucose Monitors (CGMs) can optimize your healthspan. Understand Medicare's coverage and practical steps for better metabolic health.

item.title: Beyond the Lab Report: AI, Blood Work, and Your Healthspan
item.dc:creator: health_agent
item.content:encoded:
Decoding Your Body's Story with AI

For decades, our annual physical and its accompanying blood work have been cornerstones of health monitoring. Yet, how many of us truly understand the intricate story those numbers tell? We get a report, a few highlights from our doctor, and often, that's it. But what if there was a way to dig deeper, to uncover hidden patterns and proactive steps for a longer, healthier life?

This is precisely the frontier many are now exploring. A recent Wall Street Journal article highlighted an individual's journey into this new landscape, detailing their experience of uploading personal blood work data to an AI platform for analysis. This isn't just a tech trend; it's a glimpse into the future of personalized health, empowering us to become more active participants in our longevity journey.

The Longevity Lesson: Beyond the 'Normal' Range

Traditional blood test results often focus on whether your markers fall within a broad 'normal' range. While essential for diagnosing acute conditions, this approach can sometimes overlook subtle shifts that, over time, can impact your healthspan – the period of your life spent in good health. This is where the power of AI truly shines.

Artificial intelligence can analyze vast datasets, comparing your individual blood markers not just to a static 'normal' range, but to optimal ranges tailored for longevity, your age, genetic predispositions, and lifestyle factors. It can identify correlations and potential risks that might be missed by the human eye or standard diagnostic protocols. Imagine understanding that a slightly elevated fasting glucose, while technically 'normal,' might be trending towards insulin resistance given your family history and activity levels. Or perhaps, identifying nutrient deficiencies that could impact energy and cognitive function before they become significant issues. This proactive, personalized insight is the core optimization tactic for enhancing your healthspan.

How to Improve Your Healthspan Today

While the prospect of AI analyzing your blood work is exciting, there are practical, actionable steps you can take right now to lay a strong foundation for a longer, more vibrant life:

  • Get Comprehensive Blood Panels Regularly: Don't just settle for the basics. Discuss with your doctor which advanced markers (like hs-CRP, Lp(a), Vitamin D, advanced lipid panels, fasting insulin, homocysteine, and full thyroid panels) are relevant for your age and health goals. These provide a much richer picture of your metabolic, inflammatory, and hormonal health.

  • Embrace Health Data (Wisely): Explore reputable wearables and health tracking apps that monitor sleep, activity, and heart rate variability. While AI blood analysis tools are emerging, remember they are best used for *insights* and discussion with your medical professional, not for self-diagnosis or replacing qualified medical advice. Always prioritize data privacy and security when using any online health platform.

  • Prioritize Foundational Health Habits: No amount of advanced tech can replace the impact of consistent, healthy living. Focus on a nutrient-dense diet rich in whole foods, engage in regular physical activity (a mix of cardio, strength, and flexibility), prioritize 7-9 hours of quality sleep, and manage stress effectively. These are the non-negotiable pillars of a long and vital healthspan.

The convergence of personal health data and AI offers an unprecedented opportunity to understand our bodies like never before. By embracing these advancements and combining them with time-tested healthy habits, we can move towards a future where optimal health and vitality are not just aspirations, but achievable realities for years to come.

Source: Read the original article
item.excerpt:encoded: Explore how AI is transforming personal health, making blood work insights more accessible for adults 40+. Discover practical steps for a longer, more vibrant life.

item.content:encoded:
Our company

We care about our customers the information provided on this blog is for general informational and educational purposes only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition or health-related objectives.

If you have any underlying health conditions, food allergies, or are pregnant, please consult with a healthcare provider before making changes to your diet, trying new supplements, or following any health recommendations discussed on this blog.

Stay connected

Be social with us to stay up to date on great ways to maximize your health, from great ways to eat, play and just live life to the fullest.
